org.apache.kafka.common.config.ConfigDef.Type.PASSWORD;\\n40: import static org.apache.kafka.common.config.ConfigDef.Type.SHORT;\\n41: import static org.apache.kafka.common.config.ConfigDef.Type.STRING;\\n42: \\n43: /**\\n44:  * Common home for broker-side security configs which need to be accessible from the libraries shared\\n45:  * between the broker and the client.\\n46:  *\\n47:  * Note this is an internal API and subject to change without notice.\\n48:  */\\n49: public class BrokerSecurityConfigs {\\n50: \\n51:     public static final String PRINCIPAL_BUILDER_CLASS_CONFIG = \\\"principal.builder.class\\\";\\n52: \\n53:     public static final String SSL_PRINCIPAL_MAPPING_RULES_CONFIG = \\\"ssl.principal.mapping.rules\\\";\\n54:     public static final String DEFAULT_SSL_PRINCIPAL_MAPPING_RULES = \\\"DEFAULT\\\";\\n55:     public static final String SSL_PRINCIPAL_MAPPING_RULES_DOC = \\\"A list of rules for mapping from distinguished name\\\" +\\n56:             \\\" from the client certificate to short name. The rules are evaluated in order and the first rule that matches\\\" +\\n57:             \\\" a principal name is used to map it to a short name. Any later rules in the list are ignored. By default,\\\" +\\n58:             \\\" distinguished name of the X.500 certificate will be the principal. For more details on the format please\\\" +\\n59:             \\\" see <a href=\\\\\\\"#security_authz\\\\\\\"> security authorization and acls</a>. Note that this configuration is ignored\\\" +\\n60:             \\\" if an extension of KafkaPrincipalBuilder is provided by the <code>\\\" + PRINCIPAL_BUILDER_CLASS_CONFIG + \\\"</code>\\\" +\\n61:             \\\" configuration.\\\";\\n62: \\n63:     public static final String SASL_KERBEROS_PRINCIPAL_TO_LOCAL_RULES_CONFIG = \\\"sasl.kerberos.principal.to.local.rules\\\";\\n64:     public static final List<String> DEFAULT_SASL_KERBEROS_PRINCIPAL_TO_LOCAL_RULES = Collections.singletonList(DEFAULT_SSL_PRINCIPAL_MAPPING_RULES);\\n65:     public static final String SASL_KERBEROS_PRINCIPAL_TO_LOCAL_RULES_DOC = \\\"A list of rules for mapping from principal \\\" +\\n66:             \\\"names to short names (typically operating system usernames). The rules are evaluated in order and the \\\" +\\n67:             \\\"first rule that matches a principal name is used to map it to a short name. Any later rules in the list are \\\" +\\n68:             \\\"ignored. By default, principal names of the form <code>{username}/{hostname}@{REALM}</code> are mapped \\\" +\\n69:             \\\"to <code>{username}</code>. For more details on the format please see <a href=\\\\\\\"#security_authz\\\\\\\"> \\\" +\\n70:             \\\"security authorization and acls</a>. Note that this configuration is ignored if an extension of \\\" +\\n71:             \\\"<code>KafkaPrincipalBuilder</code> is provided by the <code>\\\" + PRINCIPAL_BUILDER_CLASS_CONFIG + \\\"</code> configuration.\\\";\\n72: \\n73:     public static final Class<? extends KafkaPrincipalBuilder> PRINCIPAL_BUILDER_CLASS_DEFAULT = DefaultKafkaPrincipalBuilder.class;\\n74:     public static final String PRINCIPAL_BUILDER_CLASS_DOC = \\\"The fully qualified name of a class that implements the \\\" +\\n75:             \\\"KafkaPrincipalBuilder interface, which is used to build the KafkaPrincipal object used during \\\" +\\n76:             \\\"authorization. If no principal builder is defined, the default behavior depends \\\" +\\n77:             \\\"on the security protocol in use. For SSL authentication,  the principal will be derived using the \\\" +\\n78:             \\\"rules defined by <code>\\\" + SSL_PRINCIPAL_MAPPING_RULES_CONFIG + \\\"</code> applied on the distinguished \\\" +\\n79:             \\\"name from the client certificate if one is provided; otherwise, if client authentication is not required, \\\" +\\n80:             \\\"the principal name will be ANONYMOUS. For SASL authentication, the principal will be derived using the \\\" +\\n81:             \\\"rules defined by <code>\\\" + SASL_KERBEROS_PRINCIPAL_TO_LOCAL_RULES_CONFIG + \\\"</code> if GSSAPI is in use, \\\" +\\n82:             \\\"and the SASL authentication ID for other mechanisms. For PLAINTEXT, the principal will be ANONYMOUS. \\\" +\\n83:             \\\"Note that custom implementations of <code>KafkaPrincipalBuilder</code> is required to implement <code>KafkaPrincipalSerde</code> \\\" +\\n84:             \\\"interface, otherwise brokers will not be able to forward requests to the controller.\\\";\\n85: \\n86:     public static final String SSL_CLIENT_AUTH_CONFIG = \\\"ssl.client.auth\\\";\\n87:     public static final String SSL_CLIENT_AUTH_DEFAULT = SslClientAuth.NONE.toString();\\n88:     public static final String SSL_CLIENT_AUTH_DOC = \\\"Configures kafka broker to request client authentication.\\\"\\n89:             + \\\" The following settings are common: \\\"\\n90:             + \\\" <ul>\\\"\\n91:             + \\\" <li><code>ssl.client.auth=required</code> If set to required client authentication is required.\\\"\\n92:             + \\\" <li><code>ssl.client.auth=requested</code> This means client authentication is optional.\\\"\\n93:             + \\\" unlike required, if this option is set client can choose not to provide authentication information about itself\\\"\\n94:             + \\\" <li><code>ssl.client.auth=none</code> This means client authentication is not needed.\\\"\\n95:             + \\\"</ul>\\\";\\n96: \\n97:     public static final String SASL_ENABLED_MECHANISMS_CONFIG = \\\"sasl.enabled.mechanisms\\\";\\n98:     public static final List<String> DEFAULT_SASL_ENABLED_MECHANISMS = Collections.singletonList(SaslConfigs.GSSAPI_MECHANISM);\\n99:     public static final String SASL_ENABLED_MECHANISMS_DOC = \\\"The list of SASL mechanisms enabled in the Kafka server. \\\"\\n100:             + \\\"The list may contain any mechanism for which a security provider is available. \\\"\\n101:             + \\\"Only GSSAPI is enabled by default.\\\";\\n102: \\n103:     public static final String SASL_SERVER_CALLBACK_HANDLER_CLASS_CONFIG = \\\"sasl.server.callback.handler.class\\\";\\n104:     public static final String SASL_SERVER_CALLBACK_HANDLER_CLASS_DOC = \\\"The fully qualified name of a SASL server callback handler \\\"\\n105:             + \\\"class that implements the AuthenticateCallbackHandler interface. Server callback handlers must be prefixed with \\\"\\n106:             + \\\"listener prefix and SASL mechanism name in lower-case. For example, \\\"\\n107:             + \\\"listener.name.sasl_ssl.plain.sasl.server.callback.handler.class=com.example.CustomPlainCallbackHandler.\\\";\\n108: \\n109:     public static final String CONNECTIONS_MAX_REAUTH_MS_CONFIG = \\\"connections.max.reauth.ms\\\";\\n110:     public static final long DEFAULT_CONNECTIONS_MAX_REAUTH_MS = 0L;\\n111:     public static final String CONNECTIONS_MAX_REAUTH_MS_DOC = \\\"When explicitly set to a positive number (the default is 0, not a positive number), \\\"\\n112:             + \\\"a session lifetime that will not exceed the configured value will be communicated to v2.2.0 or later clients when they authenticate. \\\"\\n113:             + \\\"The broker will disconnect any such connection that is not re-authenticated within the session lifetime and that is then subsequently \\\"\\n114:             + \\\"used for any purpose other than re-authentication. Configuration names can optionally be prefixed with listener prefix and SASL \\\"\\n115:             + \\\"mechanism name in lower-case. For example, listener.name.sasl_ssl.oauthbearer.connections.max.reauth.ms=3600000\\\";\\n116: \\n117:     public static final String SASL_SERVER_MAX_RECEIVE_SIZE_CONFIG = \\\"sasl.server.max.receive.size\\\";\\n118:     public static final int DEFAULT_SASL_SERVER_MAX_RECEIVE_SIZE = 524288;\\n119:     public static final String SASL_SERVER_MAX_RECEIVE_SIZE_DOC = \\\"The maximum receive size allowed before and during initial SASL authentication.\\\" +\\n120:             \\\" Default receive size is 512KB. GSSAPI limits requests to 64K, but we allow upto 512KB by default for custom SASL mechanisms. In practice,\\\" +\\n121:             \\\" PLAIN, SCRAM and OAUTH mechanisms can use much smaller limits.\\\";\\n122: \\n123:     public static final String SSL_ALLOW_DN_CHANGES_CONFIG = \\\"ssl.allow.dn.changes\\\";\\n124:     public static final boolean DEFAULT_SSL_ALLOW_DN_CHANGES_VALUE = false;\\n125:     public static final String SSL_ALLOW_DN_CHANGES_DOC = \\\"Indicates whether changes to the certificate distinguished name should be allowed during\\\" +\\n126:             \\\" a dynamic reconfiguration of certificates or not.\\\";\\n127: \\n128:     public static final String SSL_ALLOW_SAN_CHANGES_CONFIG = \\\"ssl.allow.san.changes\\\";\\n129:     public static final boolean DEFAULT_SSL_ALLOW_SAN_CHANGES_VALUE = false;\\n130:     public static final String SSL_ALLOW_SAN_CHANGES_DOC = \\\"Indicates whether changes to the certificate subject alternative names should be allowed during \\\" +\\n131:             \\\"a dynamic reconfiguration of certificates or not.\\\";\\n132: \\n133:     public static final String SASL_MECHANISM_INTER_BROKER_PROTOCOL_CONFIG = \\\"sasl.mechanism.inter.broker.protocol\\\";\\n134:     public static final String SASL_MECHANISM_INTER_BROKER_PROTOCOL_DOC = \\\"SASL mechanism used for inter-broker communication. Default is GSSAPI.\\\";\\n135: \\n136:     // The allowlist of the SASL OAUTHBEARER endpoints\\n137:     public static final String ALLOWED_SASL_OAUTHBEARER_URLS_CONFIG = \\\"org.apache.kafka.sasl.oauthbearer.allowed.urls\\\";\\n138:     public static final String ALLOWED_SASL_OAUTHBEARER_URLS_DEFAULT = \\\"\\\";\\n139: \\n140:     public static final String MEDIUM, SslConfigs.SSL_KEYSTORE_PASSWORD_DOC)\\n161:             .define(SslConfigs.SSL_KEY_PASSWORD_CONFIG, PASSWORD, null, MEDIUM, SslConfigs.SSL_KEY_PASSWORD_DOC)\\n162: MEDIUM, SslConfigs.SSL_KEYSTORE_CERTIFICATE_CHAIN_DOC)\\n164:             .define(SslConfigs.SSL_TRUSTSTORE_TYPE_CONFIG, STRING, SslConfigs.DEFAULT_SSL_TRUSTSTORE_TYPE, MEDIUM, SslConfigs.SSL_TRUSTSTORE_TYPE_DOC)\\n165:             .define(SslConfigs.SSL_TRUSTSTORE_LOCATION_CONFIG, STRING, null, MEDIUM, SslConfigs.SSL_TRUSTSTORE_LOCATION_DOC)\\n166:             .define(SslConfigs.SSL_TRUSTSTORE_PASSWORD_CONFIG, PASSWORD, null, MEDIUM, SslConfigs.SSL_TRUSTSTORE_PASSWORD_DOC)\\n167:             .define(SslConfigs.SSL_TRUSTSTORE_CERTIFICATES_CONFIG, PASSWORD, null, MEDIUM, SslConfigs.SSL_TRUSTSTORE_CERTIFICATES_DOC)\\n168:             .define(SslConfigs.SSL_KEYMANAGER_ALGORITHM_CONFIG, STRING, SslConfigs.DEFAULT_SSL_KEYMANGER_ALGORITHM, MEDIUM, SslConfigs.SSL_KEYMANAGER_ALGORITHM_DOC)\\n169:             .define(SslConfigs.SSL_TRUSTMANAGER_ALGORITHM_CONFIG, STRING, SslConfigs.DEFAULT_SSL_TRUSTMANAGER_ALGORITHM, MEDIUM, SslConfigs.SSL_TRUSTMANAGER_ALGORITHM_DOC)\\n170:             .define(SslConfigs.SSL_ENDPOINT_IDENTIFICATION_ALGORITHM_CONFIG, STRING, SslConfigs.DEFAULT_SSL_ENDPOINT_IDENTIFICATION_ALGORITHM, LOW, SslConfigs.SSL_SECURE_RANDOM_IMPLEMENTATION_DOC)\\n172:             .define(SslConfigs.SSL_CIPHER_SUITES_CONFIG, LIST, Collections.emptyList(), MEDIUM, SslConfigs.SSL_CIPHER_SUITES_DOC)\\n173:             .define(SslConfigs.SSL_ENGINE_FACTORY_CLASS_CONFIG, CLASS, null, LOW, SslConfigs.SSL_ENGINE_FACTORY_CLASS_DOC)\\n174: \\n175:             // Sasl Configuration\\n176:             .define(BrokerSecurityConfigs.SASL_MECHANISM_INTER_BROKER_PROTOCOL_CONFIG, STRING, SaslConfigs.DEFAULT_SASL_MECHANISM, MEDIUM, BrokerSecurityConfigs.SASL_MECHANISM_INTER_BROKER_PROTOCOL_DOC)\\n177:             .define(BrokerSecurityConfigs.SASL_ENABLED_MECHANISMS_CONFIG, LIST, BrokerSecurityConfigs.DEFAULT_SASL_ENABLED_MECHANISMS, MEDIUM, BrokerSecurityConfigs.SASL_ENABLED_MECHANISMS_DOC)\\n178:             .define(BrokerSecurityConfigs.SASL_SERVER_CALLBACK_HANDLER_CLASS_CONFIG, CLASS, null, MEDIUM, BrokerSecurityConfigs.SASL_SERVER_CALLBACK_HANDLER_CLASS_DOC)\\n179:             .define(BrokerSecurityConfigs.SASL_KERBEROS_PRINCIPAL_TO_LOCAL_RULES_CONFIG, LIST, BrokerSecurityConfigs.DEFAULT_SASL_KERBEROS_PRINCIPAL_TO_LOCAL_RULES, MEDIUM, BrokerSecurityConfigs.SASL_KERBEROS_PRINCIPAL_TO_LOCAL_RULES_DOC)\\n180:             .define(SaslConfigs.SASL_JAAS_CONFIG, PASSWORD, null, MEDIUM, SaslConfigs.SASL_JAAS_CONFIG_DOC)\\n181:             .define(SaslConfigs.SASL_CLIENT_CALLBACK_HANDLER_CLASS, CLASS, null, MEDIUM, SaslConfigs.SASL_CLIENT_CALLBACK_HANDLER_CLASS_DOC)\\n182:             .define(SaslConfigs.SASL_LOGIN_CLASS, CLASS, null, MEDIUM, SaslConfigs.SASL_LOGIN_CLASS_DOC)\\n183:             .define(SaslConfigs.SASL_LOGIN_CALLBACK_HANDLER_CLASS, CLASS, null, MEDIUM, SaslConfigs.SASL_LOGIN_CALLBACK_HANDLER_CLASS_DOC)\\n184:             .define(SaslConfigs.SASL_KERBEROS_SERVICE_NAME, STRING, null, MEDIUM, SaslConfigs.SASL_KERBEROS_SERVICE_NAME_DOC)\\n185:             .define(SaslConfigs.SASL_KERBEROS_KINIT_CMD, STRING, SaslConfigs.DEFAULT_KERBEROS_KINIT_CMD, MEDIUM, SaslConfigs.SASL_KERBEROS_KINIT_CMD_DOC)\\n186:             .define(SaslConfigs.SASL_KERBEROS_TICKET_RENEW_WINDOW_FACTOR, DOUBLE, SaslConfigs.DEFAULT_KERBEROS_TICKET_RENEW_WINDOW_FACTOR, MEDIUM, SaslConfigs.SASL_KERBEROS_TICKET_RENEW_WINDOW_FACTOR_DOC)\\n187:             .define(SaslConfigs.SASL_KERBEROS_TICKET_RENEW_JITTER, SaslConfigs.SASL_LOGIN_CONNECT_TIMEOUT_MS_DOC)\\n194:             .define(SaslConfigs.SASL_LOGIN_READ_TIMEOUT_MS, INT, null, LOW, SaslConfigs.SASL_LOGIN_READ_TIMEOUT_MS_DOC)\\n195:    .define(SaslConfigs.SASL_OAUTHBEARER_JWT_VALIDATOR_CLASS, CLASS, SaslConfigs.DEFAULT_SASL_OAUTHBEARER_JWT_VALIDATOR_CLASS, MEDIUM, SaslConfigs.SASL_OAUTHBEARER_JWT_VALIDATOR_CLASS_DOC)\\n199:             .define(SaslConfigs.SASL_OAUTHBEARER_SCOPE, STRING, null, MEDIUM, SaslConfigs.SASL_OAUTHBEARER_SCOPE_DOC)\\n200:             .define(SaslConfigs.SASL_OAUTHBEARER_CLIENT_CREDENTIALS_CLIENT_ID, STRING, null, MEDIUM, SaslConfigs.SASL_OAUTHBEARER_CLIENT_CREDENTIALS_CLIENT_ID_DOC)\\n201:             .define(SaslConfigs.SASL_OAUTHBEARER_CLIENT_CREDENTIALS_CLIENT_SECRET, PASSWORD, null, MEDIUM, SaslConfigs.SASL_OAUTHBEARER_CLIENT_CREDENTIALS_CLIENT_SECRET_DOC)\\n202:             .define(SaslConfigs.SASL_OAUTHBEARER_ASSERTION_ALGORITHM, STRING, SaslConfigs.DEFAULT_SASL_OAUTHBEARER_ASSERTION_ALGORITHM, ConfigDef.CaseInsensitiveValidString.in(\\\"ES256\\\", \\\"RS256\\\"), MEDIUM, SaslConfigs.SASL_OAUTHBEARER_ASSERTION_ALGORITHM_DOC)\\n203: SaslConfigs.SASL_OAUTHBEARER_ASSERTION_CLAIM_EXP_SECONDS_DOC)\\n205:             .define(SaslConfigs.SASL_OAUTHBEARER_ASSERTION_CLAIM_ISS, STRING, null, MEDIUM, SaslConfigs.SASL_OAUTHBEARER_ASSERTION_CLAIM_NBF_SECONDS_DOC)\\n208:             .define(SaslConfigs.SASL_OAUTHBEARER_ASSERTION_CLAIM_SUB, STRING, null, MEDIUM, SaslConfigs.SASL_OAUTHBEARER_SCOPE_CLAIM_NAME_DOC)\\n214:             .define(SaslConfigs.SASL_OAUTHBEARER_SUB_CLAIM_NAME, STRING, SaslConfigs.DEFAULT_SASL_OAUTHBEARER_SUB_CLAIM_NAME, LOW, SaslConfigs.SASL_OAUTHBEARER_SUB_CLAIM_NAME_DOC)\\n215:             .define(SaslConfigs.SASL_OAUTHBEARER_TOKEN_ENDPOINT_URL, STRING, null, MEDIUM, SaslConfigs.SASL_OAUTHBEARER_JWKS_ENDPOINT_URL_DOC)\\n217:             .define(SaslConfigs.SASL_OAUTHBEARER_JWKS_ENDPOINT_REFRESH_MS, LONG, SaslConfigs.DEFAULT_SASL_OAUTHBEARER_JWKS_ENDPOINT_REFRESH_MS, LOW, SaslConfigs.SASL_OAUTHBEARER_JWKS_ENDPOINT_REFRESH_MS_DOC)\\n218: SaslConfigs.SASL_OAUTHBEARER_JWKS_ENDPOINT_RETRY_BACKOFF_MS_DOC)\\n219:             .define(SaslConfigs.SASL_OAUTHBEARER_JWKS_ENDPOINT_RETRY_BACKOFF_MAX_MS, LONG, SaslConfigs.DEFAULT_SASL_OAUTHBEARER_JWKS_ENDPOINT_RETRY_BACKOFF_MAX_MS, LOW, SaslConfigs.SASL_OAUTHBEARER_JWKS_ENDPOINT_RETRY_BACKOFF_MAX_MS_DOC)\\n220: STRING, null, LOW, SaslConfigs.SASL_OAUTHBEARER_EXPECTED_ISSUER_DOC);\\n223: }\\n224: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/kafka--0753c489@31d9200189f81283bf60e929ddc38fd68ac034e2/-/blob/clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/config/internals/BrokerSecurityConfigs.java\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "1: /*\n2:  * Licensed to the Apache Software Foundation (ASF) under one or more\n3:  * contributor license agreements. See the NOTICE file distributed with\n4:  * this work for additional information regarding copyright ownership.\n5:  * The ASF licenses this file to You under the Apache License, Version 2.0\n6:  * (the \"License\"); you may not use this file except in compliance with\n7:  * the License. You may obtain a copy of the License at\n8:  *\n9:  *    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n10:  *\n11:  * Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\n12:  * distributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\n13:  * WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\n14:  * See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n15:  * limitations under the License.\n16:  */\n17: package org.apache.kafka.common.config.internals;\n18: \n19: import org.apache.kafka.common.config.ConfigDef;\n20: import org.apache.kafka.common.config.SaslConfigs;\n21: import org.apache.kafka.common.config.SecurityConfig;\n22: import org.apache.kafka.common.config.SslClientAuth;\n23: import org.apache.kafka.common.config.SslConfigs;\n24: import org.apache.kafka.common.security.auth.KafkaPrincipalBuilder;\n25: import org.apache.kafka.common.security.authenticator.DefaultKafkaPrincipalBuilder;\n26: import org.apache.kafka.common.utils.Utils;\n27: \n28: import java.util.Collections;\n29: import java.util.List;\n30: \n31: import static org.apache.kafka.common.config.ConfigDef.Importance.LOW;\n32: import static org.apache.kafka.common.config.ConfigDef.Importance.MEDIUM;\n33: import static org.apache.kafka.common.config.ConfigDef.Type.BOOLEAN;\n34: import static org.apache.kafka.common.config.ConfigDef.Type.CLASS;\n35: import static org.apache.kafka.common.config.ConfigDef.Type.DOUBLE;\n36: import static org.apache.kafka.common.config.ConfigDef.Type.INT;\n37: import static org.apache.kafka.common.config.ConfigDef.Type.LIST;\n38: import static org.apache.kafka.common.config.ConfigDef.Type.LONG;\n39: import static org.apache.kafka.common.config.ConfigDef.Type.PASSWORD;\n40: import static org.apache.kafka.common.config.ConfigDef.Type.SHORT;\n41: import static org.apache.kafka.common.config.ConfigDef.Type.STRING;\n42: \n43: /**\n44:  * Common home for broker-side security configs which need to be accessible from the libraries shared\n45:  * between the broker and the client.\n46:  *\n47:  * Note this is an internal API and subject to change without notice.\n48:  */\n49: public class BrokerSecurityConfigs {\n50: \n51:     public static final String PRINCIPAL_BUILDER_CLASS_CONFIG = \"principal.builder.class\";\n52: \n53:     public static final String SSL_PRINCIPAL_MAPPING_RULES_CONFIG = \"ssl.principal.mapping.rules\";\n54:     public static final String DEFAULT_SSL_PRINCIPAL_MAPPING_RULES = \"DEFAULT\";\n55:     public static final String SSL_PRINCIPAL_MAPPING_RULES_DOC = \"A list of rules for mapping from distinguished name\" +\n56:             \" from the client certificate to short name. The rules are evaluated in order and the first rule that matches\" +\n57:             \" a principal name is used to map it to a short name. Any later rules in the list are ignored. By default,\" +\n58:             \" distinguished name of the X.500 certificate will be the principal. For more details on the format please\" +\n59:             \" see <a href=\\\"#security_authz\\\"> security authorization and acls</a>. Note that this configuration is ignored\" +\n60:             \" if an extension of KafkaPrincipalBuilder is provided by the <code>\" + PRINCIPAL_BUILDER_CLASS_CONFIG + \"</code>\" +\n61:             \" configuration.\";\n62: \n63:     public static final String SASL_KERBEROS_PRINCIPAL_TO_LOCAL_RULES_CONFIG = \"sasl.kerberos.principal.to.local.rules\";\n64:     public static final List<String> DEFAULT_SASL_KERBEROS_PRINCIPAL_TO_LOCAL_RULES = Collections.singletonList(DEFAULT_SSL_PRINCIPAL_MAPPING_RULES);\n65:     public static final String SASL_KERBEROS_PRINCIPAL_TO_LOCAL_RULES_DOC = \"A list of rules for mapping from principal \" +\n66:             \"names to short names (typically operating system usernames). The rules are evaluated in order and the \" +\n67:             \"first rule that matches a principal name is used to map it to a short name. Any later rules in the list are \" +\n68:             \"ignored. By default, principal names of the form <code>{username}/{hostname}@{REALM}</code> are mapped \" +\n69:             \"to <code>{username}</code>. For more details on the format please see <a href=\\\"#security_authz\\\"> \" +\n70:             \"security authorization and acls</a>. Note that this configuration is ignored if an extension of \" +\n71:             \"<code>KafkaPrincipalBuilder</code> is provided by the <code>\" + PRINCIPAL_BUILDER_CLASS_CONFIG + \"</code> configuration.\";\n72: \n73:     public static final Class<? extends KafkaPrincipalBuilder> PRINCIPAL_BUILDER_CLASS_DEFAULT = DefaultKafkaPrincipalBuilder.class;\n74:     public static final String PRINCIPAL_BUILDER_CLASS_DOC = \"The fully qualified name of a class that implements the \" +\n75:             \"KafkaPrincipalBuilder interface, which is used to build the KafkaPrincipal object used during \" +\n76:             \"authorization. If no principal builder is defined, the default behavior depends \" +\n77:             \"on the security protocol in use. For SSL authentication,  the principal will be derived using the \" +\n78:             \"rules defined by <code>\" + SSL_PRINCIPAL_MAPPING_RULES_CONFIG + \"</code> applied on the distinguished \" +\n79:             \"name from the client certificate if one is provided; otherwise, if client authentication is not required, \" +\n80:             \"the principal name will be ANONYMOUS. For SASL authentication, the principal will be derived using the \" +\n81:             \"rules defined by <code>\" + SASL_KERBEROS_PRINCIPAL_TO_LOCAL_RULES_CONFIG + \"</code> if GSSAPI is in use, \" +\n82:             \"and the SASL authentication ID for other mechanisms. For PLAINTEXT, the principal will be ANONYMOUS. \" +\n83:             \"Note that custom implementations of <code>KafkaPrincipalBuilder</code> is required to implement <code>KafkaPrincipalSerde</code> \" +\n84:             \"interface, otherwise brokers will not be able to forward requests to the controller.\";\n85: \n86:     public static final String SSL_CLIENT_AUTH_CONFIG = \"ssl.client.auth\";\n87:     public static final String SSL_CLIENT_AUTH_DEFAULT = SslClientAuth.NONE.toString();\n88:     public static final String SSL_CLIENT_AUTH_DOC = \"Configures kafka broker to request client authentication.\"\n89:             + \" The following settings are common: \"\n90:             + \" <ul>\"\n91:             + \" <li><code>ssl.client.auth=required</code> If set to required client authentication is required.\"\n92:             + \" <li><code>ssl.client.auth=requested</code> This means client authentication is optional.\"\n93:             + \" unlike required, if this option is set client can choose not to provide authentication information about itself\"\n94:             + \" <li><code>ssl.client.auth=none</code> This means client authentication is not needed.\"\n95:             + \"</ul>\";\n96: \n97:     public static final String SASL_ENABLED_MECHANISMS_CONFIG = \"sasl.enabled.mechanisms\";\n98:     public static final List<String> DEFAULT_SASL_ENABLED_MECHANISMS = Collections.singletonList(SaslConfigs.GSSAPI_MECHANISM);\n99:     public static final String SASL_ENABLED_MECHANISMS_DOC = \"The list of SASL mechanisms enabled in the Kafka server. \"\n100:             + \"The list may contain any mechanism for which a security provider is available. \"\n101:             + \"Only GSSAPI is enabled by default.\";\n102: \n103:     public static final String SASL_SERVER_CALLBACK_HANDLER_CLASS_CONFIG = \"sasl.server.callback.handler.class\";\n104:     public static final String SASL_SERVER_CALLBACK_HANDLER_CLASS_DOC = \"The fully qualified name of a SASL server callback handler \"\n105:             + \"class that implements the AuthenticateCallbackHandler interface. Server callback handlers must be prefixed with \"\n106:             + \"listener prefix and SASL mechanism name in lower-case. For example, \"\n107:             + \"listener.name.sasl_ssl.plain.sasl.server.callback.handler.class=com.example.CustomPlainCallbackHandler.\";\n108: \n109:     public static final String CONNECTIONS_MAX_REAUTH_MS_CONFIG = \"connections.max.reauth.ms\";\n110:     public static final long DEFAULT_CONNECTIONS_MAX_REAUTH_MS = 0L;\n111:     public static final String CONNECTIONS_MAX_REAUTH_MS_DOC = \"When explicitly set to a positive number (the default is 0, not a positive number), \"\n112:             + \"a session lifetime that will not exceed the configured value will be communicated to v2.2.0 or later clients when they authenticate. \"\n113:             + \"The broker will disconnect any such connection that is not re-authenticated within the session lifetime and that is then subsequently \"\n114:             + \"used for any purpose other than re-authentication. Configuration names can optionally be prefixed with listener prefix and SASL \"\n115:             + \"mechanism name in lower-case. For example, listener.name.sasl_ssl.oauthbearer.connections.max.reauth.ms=3600000\";\n116: \n117:     public static final String SASL_SERVER_MAX_RECEIVE_SIZE_CONFIG = \"sasl.server.max.receive.size\";\n118:     public static final int DEFAULT_SASL_SERVER_MAX_RECEIVE_SIZE = 524288;\n119:     public static final String SASL_SERVER_MAX_RECEIVE_SIZE_DOC = \"The maximum receive size allowed before and during initial SASL authentication.\" +\n120:             \" Default receive size is 512KB. GSSAPI limits requests to 64K, but we allow upto 512KB by default for custom SASL mechanisms. In practice,\" +\n121:             \" PLAIN, SCRAM and OAUTH mechanisms can use much smaller limits.\";\n122: \n123:     public static final String SSL_ALLOW_DN_CHANGES_CONFIG = \"ssl.allow.dn.changes\";\n124:     public static final boolean DEFAULT_SSL_ALLOW_DN_CHANGES_VALUE = false;\n125:     public static final String SSL_ALLOW_DN_CHANGES_DOC = \"Indicates whether changes to the certificate distinguished name should be allowed during\" +\n126:             \" a dynamic reconfiguration of certificates or not.\";\n127: \n128:     public static final String SSL_ALLOW_SAN_CHANGES_CONFIG = \"ssl.allow.san.changes\";\n129:     public static final boolean DEFAULT_SSL_ALLOW_SAN_CHANGES_VALUE = false;\n130:     public static final String SSL_ALLOW_SAN_CHANGES_DOC = \"Indicates whether changes to the certificate subject alternative names should be allowed during \" +\n131:             \"a dynamic reconfiguration of certificates or not.\";\n132: \n133:     public static final String SASL_MECHANISM_INTER_BROKER_PROTOCOL_CONFIG = \"sasl.mechanism.inter.broker.protocol\";\n134:     public static final String SASL_MECHANISM_INTER_BROKER_PROTOCOL_DOC = \"SASL mechanism used for inter-broker communication. Default is GSSAPI.\";\n135: \n136:     // The allowlist of the SASL OAUTHBEARER endpoints\n137:     public static final String ALLOWED_SASL_OAUTHBEARER_URLS_CONFIG = \"org.apache.kafka.sasl.oauthbearer.allowed.urls\";\n138:     public static final String ALLOWED_SASL_OAUTHBEARER_URLS_DEFAULT = \"\";\n139: \n140:     public static final String GSSAPI is the default mechanism.\\\";\\n32:     public static final String GSSAPI_MECHANISM = \\\"GSSAPI\\\";\\n33:     public static final String DEFAULT_SASL_MECHANISM = GSSAPI_MECHANISM;\\n34: \\n35:     public static final String SASL_JAAS_CONFIG = \\\"sasl.jaas.config\\\";\\n36:     public static final String SASL_JAAS_CONFIG_DOC = \\\"JAAS login context parameters for SASL connections in the format used by JAAS configuration files. \\\"\\n37:         + \\\"JAAS configuration file format is described <a href=\\\\\\\"https://docs.oracle.com/javase/8/docs/technotes/guides/security/jgss/tutorials/LoginConfigFile.html\\\\\\\">here</a>. \\\"\\n38: ...      *        JAAS configuration. For configs that may be specified as both Kafka config\\n39:      *        as well as JAAS config (e.g. sasl.kerberos.service.name), the configuration\\n40:      *        is treated as invalid if conflicting values are provided.\\n41:      * @param saslMechanism Negotiated SASL mechanism. For clients, this is the SASL\\n42:      *        mechanism configured for the client. For brokers, this is the mechanism\\n43:      *        negotiated with the client and is one of the mechanisms enabled on the broker.\\n44:      * @param jaasConfigEntries JAAS configuration entries from the JAAS login context.\\n45: \",\"endLine\":45,\"startLine\":38},{\"content\":\"31:     /**\\n32:      * Configures this callback handler for the specified SASL mechanism.\\n33:      *\\n34: \",\"endLine\":34,\"startLine\":31},{\"content\":\"45:      *        This list contains a single entry for clients and may contain more than\\n46:      *        one entry for brokers if multiple mechanisms are enabled on a listener using\\n47:      *        static JAAS configuration where there is no mapping between mechanisms and\\n48:      *        login module entries. In this case, callback handlers can use the login module in\\n49:      *        `jaasConfigEntries` to identify the entry corresponding to `saslMechanism`.\\n50:      *        Alternatively, dynamic JAAS configuration option\\n51:      *        {@link org.apache.kafka.common.config.SaslConfigs#SASL_JAAS_CONFIG} may be\\n52:      *        configured on brokers with listener and mechanism prefix, in which case\\n53:      *        only the configuration entry corresponding to `saslMechanism` will be provided\\n54:      *        in `jaasConfigEntries`.\\n55: \",\"endLine\":55,\"startLine\":45}],\"file\":\"clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/security/auth/AuthenticateCallbackHandler.java\",\"repo\":\"github.com/sg-evals/kafka--0753c489\",\"type\":\"file\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/kafka--0753c489/-/blob/clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/security/auth/AuthenticateCallbackHandler.java\"},{\"chunks\":[{\"content\":\"83:     private final ListenerName listenerName;\\n84:     private final boolean isInterBrokerListener;\\n85:     private final String clientSaslMechanism;\\n86:     private final ConnectionMode connectionMode;\\n87: \",\"endLine\":87,\"startLine\":83},{\"content\":\"121:         this.listenerName = listenerName;\\n122:         this.isInterBrokerListener = isInterBrokerListener;\\n123:         this.clientSaslMechanism = clientSaslMechanism;\\n124:         this.credentialCache = credentialCache;\\n125: \",\"endLine\":125,\"startLine\":121},{\"content\":\"357:     private Class<? (!OAuthBearerLoginModule.OAUTHBEARER_MECHANISM.equals(saslMechanism))\\n116:             throw new IllegalArgumentException(String.format(\\\"Unexpected SASL mechanism: %s\\\", saslMechanism));\\n117: recognizes {@link OAuthBearerExtensionsValidatorCallback} and validates every extension passed to it.\\n74:  *\\n75:  * This class is the default when the SASL mechanism is OAUTHBEARER and no value\\n76:  * is explicitly set via the\\n77:  * {@code listener.name.sasl_[plaintext|ssl].oauthbearer.sasl.server.callback.handler.class}\\n78:  * broker configuration property.\\n79:  * It is worth noting that this class is not suitable for production use due to the use of unsecured JWT tokens and\\n80:  * validation of every given extension.\\n81:  */\\n82: public class OAuthBearerUnsecuredValidatorCallbackHandler implements AuthenticateCallbackHandler {\\n83:     private static final Logger log = LoggerFactory.getLogger(OAuthBearerUnsecuredValidatorCallbackHandler.class);\\n84:     private static final String OPTION_PREFIX = \\\"unsecuredValidator\\\";\\n85:     private static final String if (callback instanceof OAuthBearerValidatorCallback) {\\n132:                 OAuthBearerValidatorCallback validationCallback = (OAuthBearerValidatorCallback) callback;\\n133:                 try {\\n134:                     handleCallback(validationCallback);\\n135:                 } catch (OAuthBearerIllegalTokenException e) {\\n136:                     OAuthBearerValidationResult failureReason = e.reason();\\n137:                     String failureScope = failureReason.failureScope();\\n138:                     validationCallback.error(failureScope != null ?         "chunks": [
          {
            "content": "53:     public static final String CONTROLLER_LISTENER_NAMES_DOC = \"A comma-separated list of the names of the listeners used by the controller. This is required \" +\n54:             \"when communicating with the controller quorum, the broker will always use the first listener in this list.\";\n55: \n56:     public static final String SASL_MECHANISM_CONTROLLER_PROTOCOL_CONFIG = \"sasl.mechanism.controller.protocol\";\n57:     public static final String SASL_MECHANISM_CONTROLLER_PROTOCOL_DOC = \"SASL mechanism used for communication with controllers. Default is GSSAPI.\";\n58: \n59:     public static final String SERVER_MAX_STARTUP_TIME_MS_CONFIG = \"server.max.startup.time.ms\";\n60:     public static final long SERVER_MAX_STARTUP_TIME_MS_DEFAULT = Long.MAX_VALUE;\n61:     public static final String SERVER_MAX_STARTUP_TIME_MS_DOC = \"The maximum number of milliseconds we will wait for the server to come up. \" +\n62:             \"By default there is no limit. This should be used for testing only.\";\n63: ",
            "endLine": 63,
            "startLine": 53
          },

GSSAPI is the default mechanism.\";\n32:     public static final String GSSAPI_MECHANISM = \"GSSAPI\";\n33:     public static final String DEFAULT_SASL_MECHANISM = GSSAPI_MECHANISM;\n34: \n35:     public static final String SASL_JAAS_CONFIG = \"sasl.jaas.config\";\n36:     public static final String SASL_JAAS_CONFIG_DOC = \"JAAS login context parameters for SASL connections in the format used by JAAS configuration files. \"\n37:         + \"JAAS configuration file format is described <a href=\\\"https://docs.oracle.com/javase/8/docs/technotes/guides/security/jgss/tutorials/LoginConfigFile.html\\\">here</a>. \"\n38: ...       {
        "chunks": [
          {
            "content": "38:      *        JAAS configuration. For configs that may be specified as both Kafka config\n39:      *        as well as JAAS config (e.g. sasl.kerberos.service.name), the configuration\n40:      *        is treated as invalid if conflicting values are provided.\n41:      * @param saslMechanism Negotiated SASL mechanism. For clients, this is the SASL\n42:      *        mechanism configured for the client. For brokers, this is the mechanism\n43:      *        negotiated with the client and is one of the mechanisms enabled on the broker.\n44:      * @param jaasConfigEntries JAAS configuration entries from the JAAS login context.\n45: ",
            "endLine": 45,
            "startLine": 38
          },
          {
            "content": "31:     /**\n32:      * Configures this callback handler for the specified SASL mechanism.\n33:      *\n34: ",
            "endLine": 34,
            "startLine": 31
          },
          {
            "content": "45:      *        This list contains a single entry for clients and may contain more than\n46:      *        one entry for brokers if multiple mechanisms are enabled on a listener using\n47:      *        static JAAS configuration where there is no mapping between mechanisms and\n48:      *        login module entries. In this case, callback handlers can use the login module in\n49:      *        `jaasConfigEntries` to identify the entry corresponding to `saslMechanism`.\n50:      *        Alternatively, dynamic JAAS configuration option\n51:      *        {@link org.apache.kafka.common.config.SaslConfigs#SASL_JAAS_CONFIG} may be\n52:      *        configured on brokers with listener and mechanism prefix, in which case\n53:      *        only the configuration entry corresponding to `saslMechanism` will be provided\n54:      *        in `jaasConfigEntries`.\n55: ",
            "endLine": 55,
            "startLine": 45
          }
        ],

            "content": "52:     @Override\n53:     public void configure(Map<String, ?> configs, String saslMechanism, List<AppConfigurationEntry> jaasConfigEntries) {\n54:         if (verificationKeyResolver.isPresent()) {\n55:             delegate = new BrokerJwtValidator(verificationKeyResolver.get());\n56:         } else {\n57:             delegate = new ClientJwtValidator();\n58:         }\n59: ",

          },
          {
            "content": "32: /**\n33:  * This {@link JwtValidator} uses the delegation approach, instantiating and delegating calls to a\n34:  * more concrete implementation. The underlying implementation is determined by the presence/absence\n35:  * of the {@link VerificationKeyResolver}: if it's present, a {@link BrokerJwtValidator} is\n36:  * created, otherwise a {@link ClientJwtValidator} is created.\n37:  */\n38: public class DefaultJwtValidator implements JwtValidator {\n39: \n40: ",
            "endLine": 40,
            "startLine": 32
          }
        ],
        "file": "clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/security/oauthbearer/DefaultJwtValidator.java",

        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/kafka--0753c489/-/blob/clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/security/oauthbearer/DefaultJwtValidator.java"
      },
      {
        "chunks": [
          {
            "content": "197:  * <p>\n198:  * Here is a typical, basic JAAS configuration for a broker leveraging unsecured\n199:  * SASL/OAUTHBEARER validation:\n200:  *\n201: ",
            "endLine": 201,
            "startLine": 197
          },
          {
            "content": "43: /**\n44:  * The {@code LoginModule} for the SASL/OAUTHBEARER mechanism. When a client\n45:  * (whether a non-broker client or a broker when SASL/OAUTHBEARER is the\n46:  * inter-broker protocol) connects to Kafka the {@code OAuthBearerLoginModule}\n47:  * instance asks its configured {@link AuthenticateCallbackHandler}\n48: ",
            "endLine": 48,
            "startLine": 43
          },
          {
            "content": "111:  * {@link OAuthBearerTokenCallback} and declaring it via either the\n112:  * {@code sasl.login.callback.handler.class} configuration option for a\n113:  * non-broker client or via the\n114:  * {@code listener.name.sasl_ssl.oauthbearer.sasl.login.callback.handler.class}\n115:  * configuration option for brokers (when SASL/OAUTHBEARER is the inter-broker\n116:  * protocol).\n117: ",
            "endLine": 117,
            "startLine": 111
          }
        ],
        "file": "clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/security/oauthbearer/OAuthBearerLoginModule.java",
        "repo": "github.com/sg-evals/kafka--0753c489",
        "type": "file",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/kafka--0753c489/-/blob/clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/security/oauthbearer/OAuthBearerLoginModule.java"
      },
      {
        "chunks": [
          {
            "content": "25:  * <ul>\n26:  *   </li>{@link SaslAuthenticationException} if SASL handshake fails with invalid credentials\n27:  *   or any other failure specific to the SASL mechanism used for authentication</li>\n28:  *   <li>{@link UnsupportedSaslMechanismException} if the SASL mechanism requested by the client\n29:  *   is not supported on the broker.</li>\n30:  *   <li>{@link IllegalSaslStateException} if an unexpected request is received on during SASL\n31:  *   handshake. This could be due to misconfigured security protocol.</li>\n32: ",
            "endLine": 32,
            "startLine": 25
          },
          {
            "content": "21: /**\n22:  * This exception indicates that SASL authentication has failed.\n23:  * On authentication failure, clients abort the operation requested and raise one\n24: ",
            "endLine": 24,
            "startLine": 21
          },
          {
            "content": "34:  */\n35: public class AuthenticationException extends InvalidConfigurationException {\n36: \n37: ",
            "endLine": 37,
            "startLine": 34
          }
        ],

            "endLine": 118,

          },
          {
            "content": "72:  * </pre>\n73:  * It also recognizes {@link OAuthBearerExtensionsValidatorCallback} and validates every extension passed to it.\n74:  *\n75:  * This class is the default when the SASL mechanism is OAUTHBEARER and no value\n76:  * is explicitly set via the\n77:  * {@code listener.name.sasl_[plaintext|ssl].oauthbearer.sasl.server.callback.handler.class}\n78:  * broker configuration property.\n79:  * It is worth noting that this class is not suitable for production use due to the use of unsecured JWT tokens and\n80:  * validation of every given extension.\n81:  */\n82: public class OAuthBearerUnsecuredValidatorCallbackHandler implements AuthenticateCallbackHandler {\n83:     private static final Logger log = LoggerFactory.getLogger(OAuthBearerUnsecuredValidatorCallbackHandler.class);\n84:     private static final String OPTION_PREFIX = \"unsecuredValidator\";\n85:     private static final String PRINCIPAL_CLAIM_NAME_OPTION = OPTION_PREFIX + \"PrincipalClaimName\";\n86: ",
            "endLine": 86,
            "startLine": 72
          },

            "content": "130:         for (Callback callback : callbacks) {\n131:             if (callback instanceof OAuthBearerValidatorCallback) {\n132:                 OAuthBearerValidatorCallback validationCallback = (OAuthBearerValidatorCallback) callback;\n133:                 try {\n134:                     handleCallback(validationCallback);\n135:                 } catch (OAuthBearerIllegalTokenException e) {\n136:                     OAuthBearerValidationResult failureReason = e.reason();\n137:                     String failureScope = failureReason.failureScope();\n138:                     validationCallback.error(failureScope != null ? \"insufficient_scope\" : \"invalid_token\",\n139:                             failureScope, failureReason.failureOpenIdConfig());\n140: ",

contextType the contextType, it must be non-null if `securityProtocol` is SASL_*; it is ignored otherwise\\n55:      * @param config client config\\n56:      * @param listenerName the listenerName if contextType is SERVER or null otherwise\\n57:      * @param clientSaslMechanism SASL mechanism if mode is CLIENT, ignored otherwise\\n58:      * @param time the time instance\\n59:      * @param logContext the log context instance\\n60:      *\\n61:      * @return the configured `ChannelBuilder`\\n62:      * @throws IllegalArgumentException if `mode` invariants described above is not maintained\\n63:      */\\n64:     public static ChannelBuilder clientChannelBuilder(\\n65:             SecurityProtocol securityProtocol,\\n66:             JaasContext.Type contextType,\\n67:             AbstractConfig config,\\n68:             ListenerName listenerName,\\n69:             String clientSaslMechanism,\\n70:             Time time,\\n71:             LogContext logContext) {\\n72: \\n73:         if (securityProtocol == SecurityProtocol.SASL_PLAINTEXT || securityProtocol == SecurityProtocol.SASL_SSL) {\\n74:             if (contextType == null)\\n75:                 throw new IllegalArgumentException(\\\"`contextType` must be non-null if `securityProtocol` is `\\\" + securityProtocol + \\\"`\\\");\\n76:             if (clientSaslMechanism == null)\\n77:                 throw new IllegalArgumentException(\\\"`clientSaslMechanism` must be non-null in client mode if `securityProtocol` is `\\\" + securityProtocol + \\\"`\\\");\\n78:         }\\n79:         return create(securityProtocol, ConnectionMode.CLIENT, contextType, config, listenerName, false, clientSaslMechanism,\\n80:             null, null, time, logContext, null);\\n81:     }\\n82: \\n83:     /**\\n84:      * @param listenerName the listenerName\\n85:      * @param isInterBrokerListener whether or not this listener is used for inter-broker requests\\n86:      * @param securityProtocol the securityProtocol\\n87:      * @param config server config\\n88:      * @param credentialCache Credential cache for SASL/SCRAM if SCRAM is enabled\\n89:      * @param tokenCache Delegation token cache\\n90:      * @param time the time instance\\n91:      * @param logContext the log context instance\\n92:      * @param apiVersionSupplier supplier for ApiVersions responses sent prior to authentication\\n93:      *\\n94:      * @return the configured `ChannelBuilder`\\n95:      */\\n96:     public static ChannelBuilder serverChannelBuilder(ListenerName listenerName,\\n97: }\\n109: \\n110:     private static ChannelBuilder create(SecurityProtocol securityProtocol,\\n111:                                          ConnectionMode connectionMode,\\n112:                                          JaasContext.Type contextType,\\n113:                                          AbstractConfig config,\\n114:                                          ListenerName listenerName,\\n115:                                          boolean isInterBrokerListener,\\n116:                                          String clientSaslMechanism,\\n117:                                     LogContext logContext,\\n121:                                          Function<Short, ApiVersionsResponse> apiVersionSupplier) {\\n122:         Map<String, Object> configs = channelBuilderConfigs(config, listenerName);\\n123: \\n124:         ChannelBuilder channelBuilder;\\n125:         switch (securityProtocol) {\\n126:             case SSL:\\n127:                 requireNonNullMode(connectionMode, securityProtocol);\\n128:                 channelBuilder = new SslChannelBuilder(connectionMode, listenerName, isInterBrokerListener, logContext);\\n129:                 break;\\n130:             case SASL_SSL:\\n131:             case SASL_PLAINTEXT:\\n132:                 requireNonNullMode(connectionMode, securityProtocol);\\n133:                 Map<String, JaasContext> jaasContexts;\\n134:                 String sslClientAuthOverride = null;\\n135:                 if (connectionMode == ConnectionMode.SERVER) {\\n136:                     @SuppressWarnings(\\\"unchecked\\\")\\n137:                     List<String> enabledMechanisms = (List<String>) configs.get(BrokerSecurityConfigs.SASL_ENABLED_MECHANISMS_CONFIG);\\n138:                     jaasContexts = new HashMap<>(enabledMechanisms.size());\\n139:                     for (String mechanism : enabledMechanisms)\\n140:                         jaasContexts.put(mechanism, JaasContext.loadServerContext(listenerName, mechanism, configs));\\n141: \\n142:                     // SSL client authentication is enabled in brokers for SASL_SSL only if listener-prefixed config is specified.\\n143:                     if (listenerName != null && securityProtocol == SecurityProtocol.SASL_SSL) {\\n144:                         String configuredClientAuth = (String) configs.get(BrokerSecurityConfigs.SSL_CLIENT_AUTH_CONFIG);\\n145:                         String listenerClientAuth = (String) config.originalsWithPrefix(listenerName.configPrefix(), true)\\n146:                                 .get(BrokerSecurityConfigs.SSL_CLIENT_AUTH_CONFIG);\\n147: \\n148:                         // If `ssl.client.auth` is configured at the listener-level, we don't set an override and SslFactory\\n149:                         // uses the value from `configs`. If not, we propagate `sslClientAuthOverride=NONE` to SslFactory and\\n150:                         // it applies the override to the latest configs when it is configured or reconfigured. `Note that\\n151:                         // ssl.client.auth` cannot be dynamically altered.\\n152:                         if (listenerClientAuth == null) {\\n153:                             sslClientAuthOverride = SslClientAuth.NONE.name().toLowerCase(Locale.ROOT);\\n154:                             if (configuredClientAuth != null && !configuredClientAuth.equalsIgnoreCase(SslClientAuth.NONE.name())) {\\n155:                                 log.warn(\\\"Broker configuration '{}' is applied only to SSL listeners. Listener-prefixed configuration can be used\\\" +\\n156:                                         \\\" to enable SSL client authentication for SASL_SSL listeners. In future releases, broker-wide option without\\\" +\\n157:                                         \\\" listener prefix may be applied to SASL_SSL listeners as well. All configuration options intended for specific\\\" +\\n158:                                         \\\" listeners should be listener-prefixed.\\\", BrokerSecurityConfigs.SSL_CLIENT_AUTH_CONFIG);\\n159:                             }\\n160:                         }\\n161:                     }\\n162:                 } else {\\n163:                     // Use server context for inter-broker client connections and client context for other clients\\n164:                     JaasContext jaasContext = contextType == JaasContext.Type.CLIENT ? JaasContext.loadClientContext(configs) :\\n165:                             JaasContext.loadServerContext(listenerName, clientSaslMechanism, configs);\\n166:                     jaasContexts = Collections.singletonMap(clientSaslMechanism, jaasContext);\\n167:                 }\\n168:                 channelBuilder = new SaslChannelBuilder(connectionMode,\\n169:                         jaasContexts,\\n170:                         securityProtocol,\\n171:                         listenerName,\\n172:                         isInterBrokerListener,\\n173:                         clientSaslMechanism,\\n174:                         credentialCache,\\n175:                         tokenCache,\\n176:                         sslClientAuthOverride,\\n177:                         time,\\n178:                         logContext,\\n179:                         apiVersionSupplier);\\n180:                 break;\\n181:             case PLAINTEXT:\\n182:                 channelBuilder = new PlaintextChannelBuilder(listenerName);\\n183:                 break;\\n184:             default:\\n185:                 throw new IllegalArgumentException(\\\"Unexpected securityProtocol \\\" + securityProtocol);\\n186:         }\\n187: \\n188:         channelBuilder.configure(configs);\\n189:         return channelBuilder;\\n190:     }\\n191: \\n192:     /**\\n193:      * @return a mutable RecordingMap. The elements got from RecordingMap are marked as \\\"used\\\".\\n194:      */\\n195:     @SuppressWarnings(\\\"unchecked\\\")\\n196:     static Map<String, Object> channelBuilderConfigs(final AbstractConfig config, final ListenerName listenerName) {\\n197:         Map<String, Object> parsedConfigs;\\n198:         if (listenerName == null)\\n199:             parsedConfigs = (Map<String, Object>) config.values();\\n200:         else\\n201:             parsedConfigs = config.valuesWithPrefixOverride(listenerName.configPrefix());\\n202: \\n203:         config.originals().entrySet().stream()\\n204:             .filter(e -> !parsedConfigs.containsKey(e.getKey())) // exclude already parsed configs\\n205:             // exclude already parsed listener prefix configs\\n206:             .filter(e -> !(listenerName != null && e.getKey().startsWith(listenerName.configPrefix()) &&\\n207:                 parsedConfigs.containsKey(e.getKey().substring(listenerName.configPrefix().length()))))\\n208:             // exclude keys like `{mechanism}.some.prop` if \\\"listener.name.\\\" prefix is present and key `some.prop` exists in parsed configs.\\n209:             .filter(e -> !(listenerName != null && parsedConfigs.containsKey(e.getKey().substring(e.getKey().indexOf('.') + 1))))\\n210:             .forEach(e -> parsedConfigs.put(e.getKey(), e.getValue()));\\n211:         return parsedConfigs;\\n212:     }\\n213: \\n214:     private static void requireNonNullMode(ConnectionMode connectionMode, SecurityProtocol securityProtocol) {\\n215:         if (connectionMode == null)\\n216:             throw new IllegalArgumentException(\\\"`mode` must be non-null if `securityProtocol` is `\\\" + securityProtocol + \\\"`\\\");\\n217:     }\\n218: \\n219:     public static KafkaPrincipalBuilder createPrincipalBuilder(Map<String, ?> configs,\\n220:                                                                KerberosShortNamer kerberosShortNamer,\\n221:                                                                SslPrincipalMapper sslPrincipalMapper) {\\n222:         Class<?> principalBuilderClass = (Class<?>) configs.get(BrokerSecurityConfigs.PRINCIPAL_BUILDER_CLASS_CONFIG);\\n223:         final KafkaPrincipalBuilder builder;\\n224: \\n225:         if (principalBuilderClass == null || principalBuilderClass == DefaultKafkaPrincipalBuilder.class) {\\n226:             builder = new DefaultKafkaPrincipalBuilder(kerberosShortNamer, sslPrincipalMapper);\\n227:         } else if (KafkaPrincipalBuilder.class.isAssignableFrom(principalBuilderClass)) {\\n228:             builder = (KafkaPrincipalBuilder) Utils.newInstance(principalBuilderClass);\\n229:         } else {\\n230:             throw new InvalidConfigurationException(\\\"Type \\\" + principalBuilderClass.getName() + \\\" is not \\\" +\\n231:                     \\\"an instance of \\\" + KafkaPrincipalBuilder.class.getName());\\n232:         }\\n233: \\n234:         if (builder instanceof Configurable)\\n235:             ((Configurable) builder).configure(configs);\\n236: \\n237:         return builder;\\n238:     }\\n239: \\n240: }\\n241: the contextType, it must be non-null if `securityProtocol` is SASL_*; it is ignored otherwise\n55:      * @param config client config\n56:      * @param listenerName the listenerName if contextType is context instance\n60:      *\n61:      * @return the configured `ChannelBuilder`\n62:      * @throws IllegalArgumentException if `mode` invariants described above is not maintained\n63:      */\n64: SecurityProtocol.SASL_PLAINTEXT || securityProtocol == SecurityProtocol.SASL_SSL) {\n74:             if (contextType == null)\n75:                 throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"`contextType` must be non-null if `securityProtocol` is `\" + securityProtocol + \"`\");\n76:             if (clientSaslMechanism == null)\n77:                 throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"`clientSaslMechanism` must be non-null in client mode if `securityProtocol` is `\" + securityProtocol + \"`\");\n78:         }\n79:         return create(securityProtocol, ConnectionMode.CLIENT, contextType, config, listenerName, false, clientSaslMechanism,\n80:             null, null, time, logContext, null);\n81:     }\n82: \n83:     /**\n84:      * @param listenerName the listenerName\n85:      * @param isInterBrokerListener whether or not this listener is used for inter-broker requests\n86:      * @param securityProtocol the securityProtocol\n87: List<String> enabledMechanisms = (List<String>) configs.get(BrokerSecurityConfigs.SASL_ENABLED_MECHANISMS_CONFIG);\n138:                     jaasContexts = new HashMap<>(enabledMechanisms.size());\n139:                     for (String mechanism : enabledMechanisms)\n140:                         jaasContexts.put(mechanism, JaasContext.loadServerContext(listenerName, mechanism, configs));\n141: \n142:                     // SSL client authentication is enabled in brokers for SASL_SSL only if listener-prefixed config is specified.\n143:                     if (listenerName != null && securityProtocol == SecurityProtocol.SASL_SSL) {\n144:                         String configuredClientAuth = (String) configs.get(BrokerSecurityConfigs.SSL_CLIENT_AUTH_CONFIG);\n145:                         String listenerClientAuth = (String) config.originalsWithPrefix(listenerName.configPrefix(), true)\n146:                                 .get(BrokerSecurityConfigs.SSL_CLIENT_AUTH_CONFIG);\n147: \n148:                         // If `ssl.client.auth` is configured at the listener-level, we don't set an override and SslFactory\n149:                         // uses the value from `configs`. If not, we propagate `sslClientAuthOverride=NONE` to SslFactory and\n150:                         // it applies the override to the latest configs when it is configured or reconfigured. `Note that\n151:                         // ssl.client.auth` cannot be dynamically altered.\n152:                         if (listenerClientAuth == null) {\n153:                             sslClientAuthOverride = SslClientAuth.NONE.name().toLowerCase(Locale.ROOT);\n154:                             if (configuredClientAuth != null && !configuredClientAuth.equalsIgnoreCase(SslClientAuth.NONE.name())) {\n155:                                 log.warn(\"Broker configuration '{}' is applied only to SSL listeners. Listener-prefixed configuration can be used\" +\n156:                                         \" to enable SSL client authentication for SASL_SSL listeners. In future releases, broker-wide option without\" +\n157:                                         \" listener prefix may be applied to SASL_SSL listeners as well. All configuration options intended for specific\" +\n158:                                         \" listeners should be listener-prefixed.\", BrokerSecurityConfigs.SSL_CLIENT_AUTH_CONFIG);\n159:                             }\n160:                         }\n161:                     }\n162:                 } else {\n163:                     // Use server context for inter-broker client connections and client context for other clients\n164:                     JaasContext jaasContext = contextType == JaasContext.Type.CLIENT ? JaasContext.loadClientContext(configs) :\n165:                             JaasContext.loadServerContext(listenerName, clientSaslMechanism, configs);\n166:                     jaasContexts = Collections.singletonMap(clientSaslMechanism, jaasContext);\n167:                 }\n168:                 channelBuilder = new SaslChannelBuilder(connectionMode,\n169:                         jaasContexts,\n170:                         securityProtocol,\n171:                         listenerName,\n172:                         isInterBrokerListener,\n173:                         clientSaslMechanism,\n174:                         credentialCache,\n175:                         tokenCache,\n176:                         sslClientAuthOverride,\n177:                         time,\n178:                         logContext,\n179:                         apiVersionSupplier);\n180:                 break;\n181:             case PLAINTEXT:\n182:                 channelBuilder = new PlaintextChannelBuilder(listenerName);\n183:                 break;\n184:             default:\n185:                 throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"Unexpected securityProtocol \" + securityProtocol);\n186:         }\n187: \n188:         channelBuilder.configure(configs);\n189:         return channelBuilder;\n190:     }\n191: \n192:     /**\n193:      * @return a mutable RecordingMap. The elements got from RecordingMap are marked as \"used\".\n194:      */\n195:     @SuppressWarnings(\"unchecked\")\n196:     static Map<String, Object> channelBuilderConfigs(final AbstractConfig config, final ListenerName listenerName) {\n197:         Map<String, Object> parsedConfigs;\n198:         if (listenerName == null)\n199:             parsedConfigs = (Map<String, Object>) config.values();\n200:         else\n201:             parsedConfigs = config.valuesWithPrefixOverride(listenerName.configPrefix());\n202: \n203:         config.originals().entrySet().stream()\n204:             .filter(e -> !parsedConfigs.containsKey(e.getKey())) // exclude already parsed configs\n205:             // exclude already parsed listener prefix configs\n206:             .filter(e -> !(listenerName != null && e.getKey().startsWith(listenerName.configPrefix()) &&\n207:                 parsedConfigs.containsKey(e.getKey().substring(listenerName.configPrefix().length()))))\n208:             // exclude keys like `{mechanism}.some.prop` if \"listener.name.\" prefix is present and key `some.prop` exists in parsed configs.\n209:             .filter(e -> !(listenerName != null && parsedConfigs.containsKey(e.getKey().substring(e.getKey().indexOf('.') + 1))))\n210:             .forEach(e -> parsedConfigs.put(e.getKey(), e.getValue()));\n211:         return parsedConfigs;\n212:     }\n213: \n214:     private static void requireNonNullMode(ConnectionMode connectionMode, SecurityProtocol securityProtocol) {\n215:         if (connectionMode == null)\n216:             throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"`mode` must be non-null if `securityProtocol` is `\" + securityProtocol + \"`\");\n217:     }\n218: \n219:     public static KafkaPrincipalBuilder createPrincipalBuilder(Map<String, ?> configs,\n220:                                                                KerberosShortNamer kerberosShortNamer,\n221:                                                                SslPrincipalMapper sslPrincipalMapper) {\n222:         Class<?> principalBuilderClass = (Class<?>) configs.get(BrokerSecurityConfigs.PRINCIPAL_BUILDER_CLASS_CONFIG);\n223:         final KafkaPrincipalBuilder builder;\n224: \n225:         if (principalBuilderClass == null || principalBuilderClass == DefaultKafkaPrincipalBuilder.class) {\n226:             builder = new DefaultKafkaPrincipalBuilder(kerberosShortNamer, sslPrincipalMapper);\n227:         } else if (KafkaPrincipalBuilder.class.isAssignableFrom(principalBuilderClass)) {\n228:             builder = (KafkaPrincipalBuilder) Utils.newInstance(principalBuilderClass);\n229:         } else {\n230:             throw new InvalidConfigurationException(\"Type \" + principalBuilderClass.getName() + \" is not \" +\n231:                     \"an instance of \" + KafkaPrincipalBuilder.class.getName());\n232:         }\n233: \n234:         if (builder instanceof Configurable)\n235:             ((Configurable) builder).configure(configs);\n236: \n237:         return builder;\n238:     }\n239: \n240: }\n241: ",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/kafka--0753c489@31d9200189f81283bf60e929ddc38fd68ac034e2/-/blob/clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/network/ChannelBuilders.java"
  }
}

13. mcp__sourcegraph__sg_read_file @ -
Input
{
  "path": "clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/security/JaasContext.java",
  "repo": "github.com/sg-evals/kafka--0753c489"
}
Output
{
  "content": "{\"content\":\"1: /*\\n2:  * Licensed to the Apache Software Foundation (ASF) under one or more\\n3:  * contributor license agreements. See the NOTICE file distributed with\\n4:  * this work for additional information regarding copyright ownership.\\n5:  * The ASF licenses this file to You under the Apache License, Version 2.0\\n6:  * (the \\\"License\\\"); you may not use this file except in compliance with\\n7:  * the License. You may obtain a copy of the License at\\n8:  *\\n9:  *    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\\n10:  *\\n11:  * Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\\n12:  * distributed under the License is distributed on an \\\"AS IS\\\" BASIS,\\n13:  * WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\\n14:  * See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\\n15:  * limitations under the License.\\n16:  */\\n17: package org.apache.kafka.common.security;\\n18: \\n19: import org.apache.kafka.common.config.SaslConfigs;\\n20: import org.apache.kafka.common.config.types.Password;\\n21: import org.apache.kafka.common.network.ListenerName;\\n22: \\n23: import org.slf4j.Logger;\\n24: import org.slf4j.LoggerFactory;\\n25: \\n26: import java.util.Arrays;\\n27: import java.util.List;\\n28: import java.util.Locale;\\n29: import java.util.Map;\\n30: import java.util.Set;\\n31: import java.util.stream.Collectors;\\n32: \\n33: import javax.security.auth.login.AppConfigurationEntry;\\n34: import javax.security.auth.login.Configuration;\\n35: \\n36: import static org.apache.kafka.common.security.JaasUtils.DISALLOWED_LOGIN_MODULES_CONFIG;\\n37: import static org.apache.kafka.common.security.JaasUtils.DISALLOWED_LOGIN_MODULES_DEFAULT;\\n38: \\n39: public class JaasContext {\\n40: \\n41:     private static final Logger LOG = LoggerFactory.getLogger(JaasContext.class);\\n42: \\n43:     private static final String GLOBAL_CONTEXT_NAME_SERVER = \\\"KafkaServer\\\";\\n44:     private static final String GLOBAL_CONTEXT_NAME_CLIENT = \\\"KafkaClient\\\";\\n45: \\n46:     /**\\n47:      * Returns an instance of this class.\\n48:      *\\n49:      * The context will contain the configuration specified by the JAAS configuration property\\n50:      * {@link SaslConfigs#SASL_JAAS_CONFIG} with prefix `listener.name.{listenerName}.{mechanism}.`\\n51:      * with listenerName and mechanism in lower case. The context `KafkaServer` will be returned\\n52:      * with a single login context entry loaded from the property.\\n53:      * <p>\\n54:      * If the property is not defined, the context will contain the default Configuration and\\n55:      * the context name will be one of:\\n56:      * <ol>\\n57:      *   <li>Lowercased listener name followed by a period and the string `KafkaServer`</li>\\n58:      *   <li>The string `KafkaServer`</li>\\n59:      *  </ol>\\n60:      * If both are valid entries in the default JAAS configuration, the first option is chosen.\\n61:      * </p>\\n62:      *\\n63:      * @throws IllegalArgumentException if listenerName or mechanism is not defined.\\n64:      */\\n65:     public static JaasContext loadServerContext(ListenerName listenerName, String mechanism, Map<String, ?> configs) {\\n66:         if (listenerName == null)\\n67:             throw new IllegalArgumentException(\\\"listenerName should not be null for SERVER\\\");\\n68:         if (mechanism == null)\\n69:             throw new IllegalArgumentException(\\\"mechanism should not be null for SERVER\\\");\\n70:         String listenerContextName = listenerName.value().toLowerCase(Locale.ROOT) + \\\".\\\" + GLOBAL_CONTEXT_NAME_SERVER;\\n71:         Password dynamicJaasConfig = (Password) configs.get(mechanism.toLowerCase(Locale.ROOT) + \\\".\\\" + SaslConfigs.SASL_JAAS_CONFIG);\\n72:         if (dynamicJaasConfig == null && configs.get(SaslConfigs.SASL_JAAS_CONFIG) != null)\\n73:             LOG.warn(\\\"Server config {} should be prefixed with SASL mechanism name, ignoring config\\\", SaslConfigs.SASL_JAAS_CONFIG);\\n74:         return load(Type.SERVER, listenerContextName, GLOBAL_CONTEXT_NAME_SERVER, dynamicJaasConfig);\\n75:     }\\n76: \\n77:     /**\\n78:      * Returns an instance of this class.\\n79:      *\\n80:      * If JAAS configuration property {@link SaslConfigs#SASL_JAAS_CONFIG} is specified,\\n81:      * the configuration object is created by parsing the property value. Otherwise, the default Configuration\\n82:      * is returned. The context name is always `KafkaClient`.\\n83:      *\\n84:      */\\n85:     public static JaasContext loadClientContext(Map<String, ?> configs) {\\n86:         Password dynamicJaasConfig = (Password) configs.get(SaslConfigs.SASL_JAAS_CONFIG);\\n87:         return load(JaasContext.Type.CLIENT, null, GLOBAL_CONTEXT_NAME_CLIENT, dynamicJaasConfig);\\n88:     }\\n89: \\n90:     static JaasContext load(JaasContext.Type contextType, String listenerContextName,\\n91:                             String globalContextName, Password dynamicJaasConfig) {\\n92:         if (dynamicJaasConfig != null) {\\n93:             JaasConfig jaasConfig = new JaasConfig(globalContextName, dynamicJaasConfig.value());\\n94:             AppConfigurationEntry[] contextModules = jaasConfig.getAppConfigurationEntry(globalContextName);\\n95:             if (contextModules == null || contextModules.length == 0)\\n96:                 throw new IllegalArgumentException(\\\"JAAS config property does not contain any login modules\\\");\\n97:             else if (contextModules.length != 1)\\n98:                 throw new IllegalArgumentException(\\\"JAAS config property contains \\\" + contextModules.length + \\\" login modules, should be 1 module\\\");\\n99: \\n100:             throwIfLoginModuleIsNotAllowed(contextModules[0]);\\n101:             return new JaasContext(globalContextName, contextType, jaasConfig, dynamicJaasConfig);\\n102:         } else\\n103:             return defaultContext(contextType, listenerContextName, globalContextName);\\n104:     }\\n105: \\n106:     private static void throwIfLoginModuleIsNotAllowed(AppConfigurationEntry appConfigurationEntry) {\\n107:         Set<String> disallowedLoginModuleList = Arrays.stream(\\n108:                 System.getProperty(DISALLOWED_LOGIN_MODULES_CONFIG, DISALLOWED_LOGIN_MODULES_DEFAULT).split(\\\",\\\"))\\n109:                 .map(String::trim)\\n110:                 .collect(Collectors.toSet());\\n111:         String loginModuleName = appConfigurationEntry.getLoginModuleName().trim();\\n112:         if (disallowedLoginModuleList.contains(loginModuleName)) {\\n113:             throw new IllegalArgumentException(loginModuleName + \\\" is not allowed. Update System property '\\\"\\n114:                     + DISALLOWED_LOGIN_MODULES_CONFIG + \\\"' to allow \\\" + loginModuleName);\\n115:         }\\n116:     }\\n117: \\n118:     private static JaasContext defaultContext(JaasContext.Type contextType, String listenerContextName,\\n119:                                               String globalContextName) {\\n120:         String jaasConfigFile = System.getProperty(JaasUtils.JAVA_LOGIN_CONFIG_PARAM);\\n121:         if (jaasConfigFile == null) {\\n122:             if (contextType == Type.CLIENT) {\\n123:                 LOG.debug(\\\"System property '\\\" + JaasUtils.JAVA_LOGIN_CONFIG_PARAM + \\\"' and Kafka SASL property '\\\" +\\n124:                         SaslConfigs.SASL_JAAS_CONFIG + \\\"' are not set, using default JAAS configuration.\\\");\\n125:             } else {\\n126:                 LOG.debug(\\\"System property '\\\" + JaasUtils.JAVA_LOGIN_CONFIG_PARAM + \\\"' is not set, using default JAAS \\\" +\\n127:                         \\\"configuration.\\\");\\n128:             }\\n129:         }\\n130: \\n131:         Configuration jaasConfig = Configuration.getConfiguration();\\n132: \\n133:         AppConfigurationEntry[] configEntries = null;\\n134:         String contextName = globalContextName;\\n135: \\n136:         if (listenerContextName != null) {\\n137:             configEntries = jaasConfig.getAppConfigurationEntry(listenerContextName);\\n138:             if (configEntries != null)\\n139:                 contextName = listenerContextName;\\n140:         }\\n141: \\n142:         if (configEntries == null)\\n143:             configEntries = jaasConfig.getAppConfigurationEntry(globalContextName);\\n144: \\n145:         if (configEntries == null) {\\n146:             String listenerNameText = listenerContextName == null ? \\\"\\\" : \\\" or '\\\" + listenerContextName + \\\"'\\\";\\n147:             String errorMessage = \\\"Could not find a '\\\" + globalContextName + \\\"'\\\" + listenerNameText + \\\" entry in the JAAS \\\" +\\n148:                     \\\"configuration. System property '\\\" + JaasUtils.JAVA_LOGIN_CONFIG_PARAM + \\\"' is \\\" +\\n149:                     (jaasConfigFile == null ? \\\"not set\\\" : jaasConfigFile);\\n150:             throw new IllegalArgumentException(errorMessage);\\n151:         }\\n152: \\n153:         for (AppConfigurationEntry appConfigurationEntry : configEntries) {\\n154:             throwIfLoginModuleIsNotAllowed(appConfigurationEntry);\\n155:         }\\n156:         return new JaasContext(contextName, contextType, jaasConfig, null);\\n157:     }\\n158: \\n159:     /**\\n160:      * The type of the SASL login context, it should be SERVER for the broker and CLIENT for the clients (consumer, producer,\\n161:      * etc.). This is used to validate behaviour (e.g. some functionality is only available in the broker or clients).\\n162:      */\\n163:     public enum Type { CLIENT, SERVER }\\n164: \\n165:     private final String name;\\n166:     private final Type type;\\n167:     private final Configuration configuration;\\n168:     private final List<AppConfigurationEntry> configurationEntries;\\n169:     private final Password dynamicJaasConfig;\\n170: \\n171:     public JaasContext(String name, Type type, Configuration configuration, Password dynamicJaasConfig) {\\n172:         this.name = name;\\n173:         this.type = type;\\n174:         this.configuration = configuration;\\n175:         AppConfigurationEntry[] entries = configuration.getAppConfigurationEntry(name);\\n176:         if (entries == null)\\n177:             throw new IllegalArgumentException(\\\"Could not find a '\\\" + name + \\\"' entry in this JAAS configuration.\\\");\\n178:         this.configurationEntries = List.of(entries);\\n179:         this.dynamicJaasConfig = dynamicJaasConfig;\\n180:     }\\n181: \\n182:     public String name() {\\n183:         return name;\\n184:     }\\n185: \\n186:     public Type type() {\\n187:         return type;\\n188:     }\\n189: \\n190:     public Configuration configuration() {\\n191:         return configuration;\\n192:     }\\n193: \\n194:     public List<AppConfigurationEntry> configurationEntries() {\\n195:         return configurationEntries;\\n196:     }\\n197: \\n198:     public Password dynamicJaasConfig() {\\n199:         return dynamicJaasConfig;\\n200:     }\\n201: \\n202:     /**\\n203:      * Returns the configuration option for <code>key</code> from this context.\\n204:      * If login module name is specified, return option value only from that module.\\n205:      */\\n206:     public static String configEntryOption(List<AppConfigurationEntry> configurationEntries, String key, String loginModuleName) {\\n207: continue;\\n210:             Object val = entry.getOptions().get(key);\\n211:             if (val != null)\\n212:                 return (String) val;\\n213:         }\\n214:         return null;\\n215:     }\\n216: \\n217: }\\n218:
