    "content": "1: /*\n2:  * Licensed to the Apache Software Foundation (ASF) under one or more\n3:  * contributor license agreements. See the NOTICE file distributed with\n4:  * this work for additional information regarding copyright ownership.\n5:  * The ASF licenses this file to You under the Apache License, Version 2.0\n6:  * (the \"License\"); you may not use this file except in compliance with\n7:  * the License. You may obtain a copy of the License at\n8:  *\n9:  *    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n10:  *\n11:  * Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\n12:  * distributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\n13:  * WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\n14:  * See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n15:  * limitations under the License.\n16:  */\n17: package org.apache.kafka.common.security;\n18: \n19: import org.apache.kafka.common.config.SaslConfigs;\n20: import org.apache.kafka.common.config.types.Password;\n21: import org.apache.kafka.common.network.ListenerName;\n22: \n23: import org.slf4j.Logger;\n24: import org.slf4j.LoggerFactory;\n25: \n26: import java.util.Arrays;\n27: import java.util.List;\n28: import java.util.Locale;\n29: import java.util.Map;\n30: import java.util.Set;\n31: import java.util.stream.Collectors;\n32: \n33: import javax.security.auth.login.AppConfigurationEntry;\n34: import javax.security.auth.login.Configuration;\n35: \n36: import static org.apache.kafka.common.security.JaasUtils.DISALLOWED_LOGIN_MODULES_CONFIG;\n37: import static org.apache.kafka.common.security.JaasUtils.DISALLOWED_LOGIN_MODULES_DEFAULT;\n38: \n39: public class JaasContext {\n40: \n41:     private static final Logger LOG = LoggerFactory.getLogger(JaasContext.class);\n42: \n43:     private static final String GLOBAL_CONTEXT_NAME_SERVER = \"KafkaServer\";\n44:     private static final String GLOBAL_CONTEXT_NAME_CLIENT = \"KafkaClient\";\n45: \n46:     /**\n47:      * Returns an instance of this class.\n48:      *\n49:      * The context will contain the configuration specified by the JAAS configuration property\n50:      * {@link SaslConfigs#SASL_JAAS_CONFIG} with prefix `listener.name.{listenerName}.{mechanism}.`\n51:      * with listenerName and mechanism in lower case. The context `KafkaServer` will be returned\n52:      * with a single login context entry loaded from the property.\n53:      * <p>\n54:      * If the property is not defined, the context will contain the default Configuration and\n55:      * the context name will be one of:\n56:      * <ol>\n57:      *   <li>Lowercased listener name followed by a period and the string `KafkaServer`</li>\n58:      *   <li>The string `KafkaServer`</li>\n59:      *  </ol>\n60:      * If both are valid entries in the default JAAS configuration, the first option is chosen.\n61:      * </p>\n62:      *\n63:      * @throws IllegalArgumentException if listenerName or mechanism is not defined.\n64:      */\n65:     public static JaasContext loadServerContext(ListenerName listenerName, String mechanism, Map<String, ?> configs) {\n66:         if (listenerName == null)\n67:             throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"listenerName should not be null for SERVER\");\n68:         if (mechanism == null)\n69:             throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"mechanism should not be null for SERVER\");\n70:         String listenerContextName = listenerName.value().toLowerCase(Locale.ROOT) + \".\" + GLOBAL_CONTEXT_NAME_SERVER;\n71:         Password dynamicJaasConfig = (Password) configs.get(mechanism.toLowerCase(Locale.ROOT) + \".\" + SaslConfigs.SASL_JAAS_CONFIG);\n72:         if (dynamicJaasConfig == null && configs.get(SaslConfigs.SASL_JAAS_CONFIG) != null)\n73:             LOG.warn(\"Server config {} should be prefixed with SASL mechanism name, ignoring config\", SaslConfigs.SASL_JAAS_CONFIG);\n74:         return load(Type.SERVER, listenerContextName, GLOBAL_CONTEXT_NAME_SERVER, dynamicJaasConfig);\n75:     }\n76: \n77:     /**\n78:      * Returns an instance of this class.\n79:      *\n80:      * If JAAS configuration property {@link SaslConfigs#SASL_JAAS_CONFIG} is specified,\n81:      * the configuration object is created by parsing the property value. Otherwise, the default Configuration\n82:      * is returned. The context name is always `KafkaClient`.\n83:      *\n84:      */\n85:     public static JaasContext loadClientContext(Map<String, ?> configs) {\n86:         Password dynamicJaasConfig = (Password) configs.get(SaslConfigs.SASL_JAAS_CONFIG);\n87:         return load(JaasContext.Type.CLIENT, null, GLOBAL_CONTEXT_NAME_CLIENT, dynamicJaasConfig);\n88:     }\n89: \n90:     static JaasContext load(JaasContext.Type contextType, String listenerContextName,\n91:                             String globalContextName, Password dynamicJaasConfig) {\n92:         if (dynamicJaasConfig != null) {\n93:             JaasConfig jaasConfig = new JaasConfig(globalContextName, dynamicJaasConfig.value());\n94:             AppConfigurationEntry[] contextModules = jaasConfig.getAppConfigurationEntry(globalContextName);\n95:             if (contextModules == null || contextModules.length == 0)\n96:                 throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"JAAS config property does not contain any login modules\");\n97:             else if (contextModules.length != 1)\n98:                 throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"JAAS config property contains \" + contextModules.length + \" login modules, should be 1 module\");\n99: \n100:             throwIfLoginModuleIsNotAllowed(contextModules[0]);\n101:             return new JaasContext(globalContextName, contextType, jaasConfig, dynamicJaasConfig);\n102:         } else\n103:             return defaultContext(contextType, listenerContextName, globalContextName);\n104:     }\n105: \n106:     private static void throwIfLoginModuleIsNotAllowed(AppConfigurationEntry appConfigurationEntry) {\n107:         Set<String> disallowedLoginModuleList = Arrays.stream(\n108:                 System.getProperty(DISALLOWED_LOGIN_MODULES_CONFIG, DISALLOWED_LOGIN_MODULES_DEFAULT).split(\",\"))\n109:                 .map(String::trim)\n110:                 .collect(Collectors.toSet());\n111:         String loginModuleName = appConfigurationEntry.getLoginModuleName().trim();\n112:         if (disallowedLoginModuleList.contains(loginModuleName)) {\n113:             throw new IllegalArgumentException(loginModuleName + \" is not allowed. Update System property '\"\n114:                     + DISALLOWED_LOGIN_MODULES_CONFIG + \"' to allow \" + loginModuleName);\n115:         }\n116:     }\n117: \n118:     private static JaasContext defaultContext(JaasContext.Type contextType, String listenerContextName,\n119:                                               String globalContextName) {\n120:         String jaasConfigFile = System.getProperty(JaasUtils.JAVA_LOGIN_CONFIG_PARAM);\n121:         if (jaasConfigFile == null) {\n122:             if (contextType == Type.CLIENT) {\n123:                 LOG.debug(\"System property '\" + JaasUtils.JAVA_LOGIN_CONFIG_PARAM + \"' and Kafka SASL property '\" +\n124:                         SaslConfigs.SASL_JAAS_CONFIG + \"' are not set, using default JAAS configuration.\");\n125:             } else {\n126:                 LOG.debug(\"System property '\" + JaasUtils.JAVA_LOGIN_CONFIG_PARAM + \"' is not set, using default JAAS \" +\n127:                         \"configuration.\");\n128:             }\n129:         }\n130: \n131:         Configuration jaasConfig = Configuration.getConfiguration();\n132: \n133:         AppConfigurationEntry[] configEntries = null;\n134:         String contextName = globalContextName;\n135: \n136:         if (listenerContextName != null) {\n137:             configEntries = jaasConfig.getAppConfigurationEntry(listenerContextName);\n138:             if (configEntries != null)\n139:                 contextName = listenerContextName;\n140:         }\n141: \n142:         if (configEntries == null)\n143:             configEntries = jaasConfig.getAppConfigurationEntry(globalContextName);\n144: \n145:         if (configEntries == null) {\n146:             String listenerNameText = listenerContextName == null ? \"\" : \" or '\" + listenerContextName + \"'\";\n147:             String errorMessage = \"Could not find a '\" + globalContextName + \"'\" + listenerNameText + \" entry in the JAAS \" +\n148:                     \"configuration. System property '\" + JaasUtils.JAVA_LOGIN_CONFIG_PARAM + \"' is \" +\n149:                     (jaasConfigFile == null ? \"not set\" : jaasConfigFile);\n150:             throw new IllegalArgumentException(errorMessage);\n151:         }\n152: \n153:         for (AppConfigurationEntry appConfigurationEntry : configEntries) {\n154:             throwIfLoginModuleIsNotAllowed(appConfigurationEntry);\n155:         }\n156:         return new JaasContext(contextName, contextType, jaasConfig, null);\n157:     }\n158: \n159:     /**\n160:      * The type of the SASL login context, it should be SERVER for the broker and CLIENT for the clients (consumer, producer,\n161:      * etc.). This is used to validate behaviour (e.g. some functionality is only available in the broker or clients).\n162:      */\n163:     public enum Type { CLIENT, SERVER }\n164: \n165:     private final String name;\n166:     private final Type type;\n167:     private final Configuration configuration;\n168:     private final List<AppConfigurationEntry> configurationEntries;\n169:     private final Password dynamicJaasConfig;\n170: \n171:     public JaasContext(String name, Type type, Configuration configuration, Password dynamicJaasConfig) {\n172:         this.name = name;\n173:         this.type = type;\n174:         this.configuration = configuration;\n175:         AppConfigurationEntry[] entries = configuration.getAppConfigurationEntry(name);\n176:         if (entries == null)\n177:             throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"Could not find a '\" + name + \"' entry in this JAAS configuration.\");\n178:         this.configurationEntries = List.of(entries);\n179:         this.dynamicJaasConfig = dynamicJaasConfig;\n180:     }\n181: \n182:     public String name() {\n183:         return name;\n184:     }\n185: \n186:     public Type type() {\n187:         return type;\n188:     }\n189: \n190:     public Configuration configuration() {\n191:         return configuration;\n192:     }\n193: \n194:     public List<AppConfigurationEntry> configurationEntries() {\n195:         return configurationEntries;\n196:     }\n197: \n198:     public Password dynamicJaasConfig() {\n199:         return dynamicJaasConfig;\n200:     }\n201: \n202:     /**\n203:      * Returns the configuration option for <code>key</code> from this context.\n204:      * If login module name is specified, return option value only from that module.\n205:      */\n206:     public static String configEntryOption(List<AppConfigurationEntry> configurationEntries, String key, String loginModuleName) {\n207:         for (AppConfigurationEntry entry : configurationEntries) {\n208:             if (loginModuleName != null && !loginModuleName.equals(entry.getLoginModuleName()))\n209:                 continue;\n210:             Object val = entry.getOptions().get(key);\n211:             if (val != null)\n212:                 return (String) val;\n213:         }\n214:         return null;\n215:     }\n216: \n217: }\n218: ",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/kafka--0753c489@31d9200189f81283bf60e929ddc38fd68ac034e2/-/blob/clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/security/JaasContext.java"
  }

            "content": "310: \n311:     static ApiError validateScramUsernameAndMechanism(\n312:         String username,\n313:         ScramMechanism mechanism\n314:     ) {\n315:         if (username.isEmpty()) {\n316:             return new ApiError(UNACCEPTABLE_CREDENTIAL, \"Username must not be empty\");\n317:         }\n318:         if (mechanism == ScramMechanism.UNKNOWN) {\n319:             return new ApiError(UNSUPPORTED_SASL_MECHANISM, \"Unknown SCRAM mechanism\");\n320:         }\n321: ",
            "endLine": 321,
            "startLine": 310
          },
          {
            "content": "81: \n82:     static class ScramCredentialKey {\n83:         private final String username;\n84:         private final ScramMechanism mechanism;\n85: \n86:         ScramCredentialKey(String username, ScramMechanism mechanism) {\n87:             this.username = username;\n88:             this.mechanism = mechanism;\n89: ",
            "endLine": 89,
            "startLine": 81
          },
          {
            "content": "297: \n298:     ApiError validateDeletion(ScramCredentialDeletion deletion) {\n299:         ApiError error = validateScramUsernameAndMechanism(deletion.name(),\n300:             ScramMechanism.fromType(deletion.mechanism()));\n301:         if (error.isFailure()) return error;\n302:         ScramCredentialKey key = new ScramCredentialKey(deletion.name(),\n303:             ScramMechanism.fromType(deletion.mechanism()));\n304:         if (!credentials.containsKey(key)) {\n305:             return new ApiError(RESOURCE_NOT_FOUND,\n306:                 \"Attempt to delete a user credential that does not exist\");\n307:         }\n308: ",
            "endLine": 308,
            "startLine": 297

  "content": "{\"content\":\"1: /*\\n2:  * Licensed to the Apache Software Foundation (ASF) under one or more\\n3:  * contributor license agreements.  See the NOTICE file distributed with\\n4:  * this work for additional information regarding copyright ownership.\\n5:  * The ASF licenses this file to You under the Apache License, Version 2.0\\n6:  * (the \\\"License\\\"); you may not use this file except in compliance with\\n7:  * the License.  You may obtain a copy of the License at\\n8:  *\\n9:  *    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\\n10:  *\\n11:  * Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\\n12:  * distributed under the License is distributed on an \\\"AS IS\\\" BASIS,\\n13:  * WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\\n14:  * See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\\n15:  * limitations under the License.\\n16:  */\\n17: \\n18: package kafka.server\\n19: \\n20: import java.util\\n21: import java.util.Properties\\n22: import org.apache.kafka.common.config.ConfigResource\\n23: import org.apache.kafka.common.config.ConfigResource.Type.{BROKER, CLIENT_METRICS, GROUP, TOPIC}\\n24: import org.apache.kafka.controller.ConfigurationValidator\\n25: import org.apache.kafka.common.errors.{InvalidConfigurationException, InvalidRequestException}\\n26: import org.apache.kafka.common.internals.Topic\\n27: import org.apache.kafka.coordinator.group.GroupConfigManager\\n28: import org.apache.kafka.server.metrics.ClientMetricsConfigs\\n29: import org.apache.kafka.storage.internals.log.LogConfig\\n30: \\n31: import scala.collection.mutable\\n32: \\n33: /**\\n34:  * The validator that the controller uses for dynamic configuration changes.\\n35:  * It performs the generic validation, which can't be bypassed. If an AlterConfigPolicy\\n36:  * is configured, the controller will check that after verifying that this passes.\\n37:  *\\n38:  * For changes to BROKER resources, the forwarding broker performs an extra validation step\\n39:  * in {@link kafka.server.ConfigAdminManager#preprocess()} before sending the change to\\n40:  * the controller. Therefore, the validation here is just a kind of sanity check, which\\n41:  * should never fail under normal conditions.\\n42:  *\\n43:  * This validator does not handle changes to BROKER_LOGGER resources. Despite being bundled\\n44:  * in the same RPC, BROKER_LOGGER is not really a dynamic configuration in the same sense\\n45:  * as the others. It is not persisted to the metadata log.\\n46:  */\\n47: class ControllerConfigurationValidator(kafkaConfig: KafkaConfig) extends ConfigurationValidator {\\n48:   private def validateTopicName(\\n49:     name: String\\n50:   ): Unit = {\\n51:     if (name.isEmpty) {\\n52:       throw new InvalidRequestException(\\\"Default topic resources are not allowed.\\\")\\n53:     }\\n54:     Topic.validate(name)\\n55:   }\\n56: \\n57:   private def validateBrokerName(\\n58:     name: String\\n59:   ): Unit = {\\n60:     if (name.nonEmpty) {\\n61:       val brokerId = try {\\n62:         Integer.valueOf(name)\\n63:       } catch {\\n64:         case _: NumberFormatException =>\\n65:           throw new InvalidRequestException(\\\"Unable to parse broker name as a base 10 number.\\\")\\n66:       }\\n67:       if (brokerId < 0) {\\n68:         throw new InvalidRequestException(\\\"Invalid negative broker ID.\\\")\\n69:       }\\n70:     }\\n71:   }\\n72: \\n73:   private def validateGroupName(\\n74:     name: String\\n75:   ): Unit = {\\n76:     if (name.isEmpty) {\\n77:       throw new InvalidRequestException(\\\"Default group resources are not allowed.\\\")\\n78:     }\\n79:   }\\n80: \\n81:   private def throwExceptionForUnknownResourceType(\\n82:     resource: ConfigResource\\n83:   ): Unit = {\\n84:     // Note: we should never handle BROKER_LOGGER resources here, since changes to\\n85:     // those resources are not persisted in the metadata.\\n86:     throw new InvalidRequestException(s\\\"Unknown resource type ${resource.`type`}\\\")\\n87:   }\\n88: \\n89:   override def validate(\\n90:     resource: ConfigResource\\n91:   ): Unit = {\\n92:     resource.`type`() match {\\n93:       case TOPIC => validateTopicName(resource.name())\\n94:       case BROKER => validateBrokerName(resource.name())\\n95:       case _ => throwExceptionForUnknownResourceType(resource)\\n96:     }\\n97:   }\\n98: \\n99:   override def validate(\\n100:     resource: ConfigResource,\\n101:     newConfigs: util.Map[String, String],\\n102:     oldConfigs: util.Map[String, String]\\n103:   ): Unit = {\\n104:     resource.`type`() match {\\n105:       case TOPIC =>\\n106:         validateTopicName(resource.name())\\n107:         val properties = new Properties()\\n108:         val nullTopicConfigs = new mutable.ArrayBuffer[String]()\\n109:         newConfigs.forEach((key, value) => {\\n110:           if (value == null) {\\n111:             nullTopicConfigs += key\\n112:           } else {\\n113:             properties.setProperty(key, value)\\n114:           }\\n115:         })\\n116:         if (nullTopicConfigs.nonEmpty) {\\n117:           throw new InvalidConfigurationException(\\\"Null value not supported for topic configs: \\\" +\\n118:             nullTopicConfigs.mkString(\\\",\\\"))\\n119:         }\\n120:         LogConfig.validate(oldConfigs, properties, kafkaConfig.extractLogConfigMap,\\n121:           kafkaConfig.remoteLogManagerConfig.isRemoteStorageSystemEnabled())\\n122:       case BROKER => validateBrokerName(resource.name())\\n123:       case CLIENT_METRICS =>\\n124:         val properties = new Properties()\\n125:         newConfigs.forEach((key, value) => properties.setProperty(key, value))\\n126:         ClientMetricsConfigs.validate(resource.name(), properties)\\n127:       case GROUP =>\\n128:         validateGroupName(resource.name())\\n129:         val properties = new Properties()\\n130:         val nullGroupConfigs = new mutable.ArrayBuffer[String]()\\n131:         newConfigs.forEach((key, value) => {\\n132:           if (value == null) {\\n133:             nullGroupConfigs += key\\n134:           } else {\\n135:             properties.setProperty(key, value)\\n136:           }\\n137:         })\\n138:         if (nullGroupConfigs.nonEmpty) {\\n139:           throw new InvalidConfigurationException(\\\"Null value not supported for group configs: \\\" +\\n140:             nullGroupConfigs.mkString(\\\",\\\"))\\n141:         }\\n142:         GroupConfigManager.validate(properties, kafkaConfig.groupCoordinatorConfig, kafkaConfig.shareGroupConfig)\\n143:       case _ => throwExceptionForUnknownResourceType(resource)\\n144:     }\\n145:   }\\n146: }\\n147: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/kafka--0753c489@31d9200189f81283bf60e929ddc38fd68ac034e2/-/blob/core/src/main/scala/kafka/server/ControllerConfigurationValidator.scala\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "1: /*\n2:  * Licensed to the Apache Software Foundation (ASF) under one or more\n3:  * contributor license agreements.  See the NOTICE file distributed with\n4:  * this work for additional information regarding copyright ownership.\n5:  * The ASF licenses this file to You under the Apache License, Version 2.0\n6:  * (the \"License\"); you may not use this file except in compliance with\n7:  * the License.  You may obtain a copy of the License at\n8:  *\n9:  *    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n10:  *\n11:  * Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\n12:  * distributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\n13:  * WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\n14:  * See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n15:  * limitations under the License.\n16:  */\n17: \n18: package kafka.server\n19: \n20: import java.util\n21: import java.util.Properties\n22: import org.apache.kafka.common.config.ConfigResource\n23: import org.apache.kafka.common.config.ConfigResource.Type.{BROKER, CLIENT_METRICS, GROUP, TOPIC}\n24: import org.apache.kafka.controller.ConfigurationValidator\n25: import org.apache.kafka.common.errors.{InvalidConfigurationException, InvalidRequestException}\n26: import org.apache.kafka.common.internals.Topic\n27: import org.apache.kafka.coordinator.group.GroupConfigManager\n28: import org.apache.kafka.server.metrics.ClientMetricsConfigs\n29: import org.apache.kafka.storage.internals.log.LogConfig\n30: \n31: import scala.collection.mutable\n32: \n33: /**\n34:  * The validator that the controller uses for dynamic configuration changes.\n35:  * It performs the generic validation, which can't be bypassed. If an AlterConfigPolicy\n36:  * is configured, the controller will check that after verifying that this passes.\n37:  *\n38:  * For changes to BROKER resources, the forwarding broker performs an extra validation step\n39:  * in {@link kafka.server.ConfigAdminManager#preprocess()} before sending the change to\n40:  * the controller. Therefore, the validation here is just a kind of sanity check, which\n41:  * should never fail under normal conditions.\n42:  *\n43:  * This validator does not handle changes to BROKER_LOGGER resources. Despite being bundled\n44:  * in the same RPC, BROKER_LOGGER is not really a dynamic configuration in the same sense\n45:  * as the others. It is not persisted to the metadata log.\n46:  */\n47: class ControllerConfigurationValidator(kafkaConfig: KafkaConfig) extends ConfigurationValidator {\n48:   private def validateTopicName(\n49:     name: String\n50:   ): Unit = {\n51:     if (name.isEmpty) {\n52:       throw new InvalidRequestException(\"Default topic resources are not allowed.\")\n53:     }\n54:     Topic.validate(name)\n55:   }\n56: \n57:   private def validateBrokerName(\n58:     name: String\n59:   ): Unit = {\n60:     if (name.nonEmpty) {\n61:       val brokerId = try {\n62:         Integer.valueOf(name)\n63:       } catch {\n64:         case _: NumberFormatException =>\n65:           throw new InvalidRequestException(\"Unable to parse broker name as a base 10 number.\")\n66:       }\n67:       if (brokerId < 0) {\n68:         throw new InvalidRequestException(\"Invalid negative broker ID.\")\n69:       }\n70:     }\n71:   }\n72: \n73:   private def validateGroupName(\n74:     name: String\n75:   ): Unit = {\n76:     if (name.isEmpty) {\n77:       throw new InvalidRequestException(\"Default group resources are not allowed.\")\n78:     }\n79:   }\n80: \n81:   private def throwExceptionForUnknownResourceType(\n82:     resource: ConfigResource\n83:   ): Unit = {\n84:     // Note: we should never handle BROKER_LOGGER resources here, since changes to\n85:     // those resources are not persisted in the metadata.\n86:     throw new InvalidRequestException(s\"Unknown resource type ${resource.`type`}\")\n87:   }\n88: \n89:   override def validate(\n90:     resource: ConfigResource\n91:   ): Unit = {\n92:     resource.`type`() match {\n93:       case TOPIC => validateTopicName(resource.name())\n94:       case BROKER => validateBrokerName(resource.name())\n95:       case _ => throwExceptionForUnknownResourceType(resource)\n96:     }\n97:   }\n98: \n99:   override def validate(\n100:     resource: ConfigResource,\n101:     newConfigs: util.Map[String, String],\n102:     oldConfigs: util.Map[String, String]\n103:   ): Unit = {\n104:     resource.`type`() match {\n105:       case TOPIC =>\n106:         validateTopicName(resource.name())\n107:         val properties = new Properties()\n108:         val nullTopicConfigs = new mutable.ArrayBuffer[String]()\n109:         newConfigs.forEach((key, value) => {\n110:           if (value == null) {\n111:             nullTopicConfigs += key\n112:           } else {\n113:             properties.setProperty(key, value)\n114:           }\n115:         })\n116:         if (nullTopicConfigs.nonEmpty) {\n117:           throw new InvalidConfigurationException(\"Null value not supported for topic configs: \" +\n118:             nullTopicConfigs.mkString(\",\"))\n119:         }\n120:         LogConfig.validate(oldConfigs, properties, kafkaConfig.extractLogConfigMap,\n121:           kafkaConfig.remoteLogManagerConfig.isRemoteStorageSystemEnabled())\n122:       case BROKER => validateBrokerName(resource.name())\n123:       case CLIENT_METRICS =>\n124:         val properties = new Properties()\n125:         newConfigs.forEach((key, value) => properties.setProperty(key, value))\n126:         ClientMetricsConfigs.validate(resource.name(), properties)\n127:       case GROUP =>\n128:         validateGroupName(resource.name())\n129:         val properties = new Properties()\n130:         val nullGroupConfigs = new mutable.ArrayBuffer[String]()\n131:         newConfigs.forEach((key, value) => {\n132:           if (value == null) {\n133:             nullGroupConfigs += key\n134:           } else {\n135:             properties.setProperty(key, value)\n136:           }\n137:         })\n138:         if (nullGroupConfigs.nonEmpty) {\n139:           throw new InvalidConfigurationException(\"Null value not supported for group configs: \" +\n140:             nullGroupConfigs.mkString(\",\"))\n141:         }\n142:         GroupConfigManager.validate(properties, kafkaConfig.groupCoordinatorConfig, kafkaConfig.shareGroupConfig)\n143:       case _ => throwExceptionForUnknownResourceType(resource)\n144:     }\n145:   }\n146: }\n147: ",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/kafka--0753c489@31d9200189f81283bf60e929ddc38fd68ac034e2/-/blob/core/src/main/scala/kafka/server/ControllerConfigurationValidator.scala"
  }

22. Agent @ -
Input
{
  "description": "Find SASL configuration startup validation code in kafka broker",
  "prompt": "Search the apache/kafka repository (github.com/sg-evals/kafka--0753c489) for Java source files that validate SASL mechanism configuration at broker startup, including:\n1. Validation of sasl.enabled.mechanisms\n2. Validation that JAAS config is present for enabled mechanisms\n3. Mechanism-specific validation (SCRAM, GSSAPI, OAUTHBEARER)\n4. Validation of inter-broker SASL mechanism\n\nFocus on:\n- Files in server/src/main/java directory\n- Files that reference sasl.enabled.mechanisms or SASL_ENABLED_MECHANISMS_CONFIG\n- Files that contain \"validate\" or \"validation\" related to SASL\n- Files related to inter-broker communication or protocol validation\n- Look for code that throws exceptions for invalid SASL configuration\n\nReturn all files that contain SASL configuration validation logic, with their file paths.",
  "subagent_type": "Explore"
}
Output
{
  "agentId": "a74fe51df0d169c6f",
  "content": [
    {
      "text": "Perfect! Now I have comprehensive information. Let me compile my findings into a complete report:\n\n## Summary\n\nI have successfully searched the Apache Kafka repository (github.com/sg-evals/kafka--0753c489) for Java source files that validate SASL mechanism configuration at broker startup. Here are all the files containing SASL configuration validation logic:\n\n### Core SASL Configuration Validation Files:\n\n1. **`/workspace/clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/security/JaasContext.java`**\n   - Validates JAAS configuration and login modules at startup\n   - Checks for null listener names and mechanisms for SERVER contexts (lines 65-75)\n   - Validates that JAAS config contains exactly one login module for dynamic configs (lines 92-104)\n   - Validates that disallowed login modules are not used (lines 106-116)\n   - Throws IllegalArgumentException when required entries are missing (lines 145-150)\n\n2. **`/workspace/clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/config/internals/BrokerSecurityConfigs.java`**\n   - Defines SASL configuration constants and validation\n   - Contains `SASL_ENABLED_MECHANISMS_CONFIG` definition (line 97)\n   - Contains `SASL_MECHANISM_INTER_BROKER_PROTOCOL_CONFIG` definition (line 133)\n   - Defines ConfigDef with SASL configuration options (lines 143-222)\n   - Specifies validators for mechanism list and configuration properties\n\n3. **`/workspace/clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/security/authenticator/SaslServerAuthenticator.java`**\n   - Validates enabled mechanisms at broker startup (lines 178-189)\n   - Checks that at least one mechanism is enabled (line 179-180)\n   - Verifies callback handlers exist for each enabled mechanism (lines 183-184)\n   - Validates Subject objects are present for each mechanism (lines 185-186)\n   - Creates SASL servers for each mechanism including GSSAPI-specific handling (lines 200-209)\n\n4. **`/workspace/clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/network/SaslChannelBuilder.java`**\n   - Server-side callback handler creation and validation (lines 317-336)\n   - Creates mechanism-specific handlers: PLAIN, SCRAM, OAUTHBEARER, GSSAPI (lines 326-333)\n   - Validates callback handlers are properly configured (lines 149-152)\n   - Handles LoginManager acquisition for each mechanism (lines 166-176)\n   - Validates GSSAPI-specific Kerberos configuration (lines 155-165, 174-175)\n\n5. **`/workspace/core/src/main/scala/kafka/server/KafkaConfig.scala`**\n   - Validates inter-broker SASL mechanism configuration (lines 627-629)\n   - Ensures `sasl.mechanism.inter.broker.protocol` is in enabled mechanisms list\n   - Throws exception if inter-broker SASL is configured but mechanism not enabled\n   - Contains `saslEnabledMechanisms()` helper method (lines 393+)\n\n6. **`/workspace/clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/clients/CommonClientConfigs.java`**\n   - Validates client SASL mechanism configuration (lines 297-306)\n   - Ensures SASL_MECHANISM is set when SASL_PLAINTEXT or SASL_SSL security protocol is used\n   - Throws ConfigException if mechanism is null or empty when SASL is enabled\n\n7. **`/workspace/core/src/main/scala/kafka/server/DynamicBrokerConfig.scala`**\n   - Lists all dynamically reconfigurable SASL configurations (lines 900-914)\n   - Includes: SASL_MECHANISM_INTER_BROKER_PROTOCOL_CONFIG, SASL_JAAS_CONFIG, SASL_ENABLED_MECHANISMS_CONFIG\n   - References Kerberos, login refresh, and SCRAM-related configs\n\n### Mechanism-Specific Implementation Files:\n\n8. **`/workspace/clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/security/plain/internals/PlainServerCallbackHandler.java`**\n   - Validates PLAIN mechanism configuration via JAAS context\n\n9. **`/workspace/clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/security/scram/internals/ScramServerCallbackHandler.java`**\n   - Handles SCRAM mechanism validation and credential lookups\n\n10. **`/workspace/clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/security/oauthbearer/internals/unsecured/OAuthBearerUnsecuredValidatorCallbackHandler.java`**\n    - Validates OAUTHBEARER configuration (lines 113-124)\n    - Ensures exactly one JAAS configuration entry is present\n    - Validates login module is OAuthBearerLoginModule\n\n11. **`/workspace/clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/security/oauthbearer/internals/secured/ConfigurationUtils.java`**\n    - Provides validation utilities for OAUTHBEARER configuration\n    - Validates file URLs and mechanism-specific configuration properties\n\n12. **`/workspace/clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/security/authenticator/LoginManager.java`**\n    - Validates mechanism-specific login configuration\n    - Checks for multiple login modules conflict with configured callbacks (lines 220-224)\n\n### Configuration Definition Files:\n\n13. **`/workspace/server/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/server/config/KRaftConfigs.java`**\n    - Defines KRaft-specific SASL mechanism configuration\n    - Contains SASL_MECHANISM_CONTROLLER_PROTOCOL_CONFIG (line 56)\n\nAll these files work together to validate SASL mechanism configuration at broker startup, including:\n- Validation that enabled mechanisms list is not empty\n- Verification of callback handler availability for each mechanism\n- Validation of JAAS configuration presence and correctness\n- Inter-broker SASL mechanism consistency checking\n- Mechanism-specific validation (SCRAM, GSSAPI, PLAIN, OAUTHBEARER)",
      "type": "text"
    }
  ],
  "prompt": "Search the apache/kafka repository (github.com/sg-evals/kafka--0753c489) for Java source files that validate SASL mechanism configuration at broker startup, including:\n1. Validation of inter-broker SASL mechanism\n\nFocus on:\n- Files in server/src/main/java directory\n- Files that reference sasl.enabled.mechanisms or SASL_ENABLED_MECHANISMS_CONFIG\n- Files that contain \"validate\" or \"validation\" related to SASL\n- Files related to inter-broker communication or protocol validation\n- Look for code that throws exceptions for invalid SASL configuration\n\nReturn all files that contain SASL configuration validation logic, with their file paths.",

23. Glob @ -
Input
{
  "path": "/workspace",
  "pattern": "**/server/src/main/java/**/*.java"
}
Output
"No files found"
24. Grep @ -
Input
{
  "path": "/workspace",
  "pattern": "SASL_ENABLED_MECHANISMS_CONFIG|sasl\\.enabled\\.mechanisms",
  "type": "java"
}
Output
"No files found"
25. Grep @ -
Input
{
  "path": "/workspace",
  "pattern": "validate.*[Ss]asl|[Ss]asl.*validate",
  "type": "java"
}
Output
"No files found"
26. Bash @ -
Input
{
  "command": "find /workspace -type f -name \"*.java\" | grep -i sasl | head -20",
  "description": "Find Java files with 'sasl' in the name"
}
Output
""

\",\"endLine\":47,\"startLine\":42},{\"content\":\"25: /**\\n26:  * An interface for enforcing a policy on overriding of Kafka client configs via the connector configs.\\n27:  * <p>\\n28:  * Common For brokers, this is the mechanism\\n43:      *        negotiated with the client and is one of the mechanisms enabled on the broker.\\n44:      * @param jaasConfigEntries JAAS configuration entries from the JAAS login context.\\n45:      *        This list contains a single entry for clients and may contain more than\\n46:      *        one entry for brokers if multiple mechanisms are enabled on a listener using\\n47:      *        static JAAS configuration where there is no mapping between mechanisms and\\n48:      *        login module entries. "{\"content\":\"1: /*\\n2:  * Licensed to the Apache Software Foundation (ASF) under one or more\\n3:  * contributor license agreements. See the NOTICE file distributed with\\n4:  * this work for additional information regarding copyright ownership.\\n5:  * The ASF licenses this file to You under the Apache License, Version 2.0\\n6:  * (the \\\"License\\\"); you may not use this file except in compliance with\\n7:  * the License. You may obtain a copy of the License at\\n8:  *\\n9:  *    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\\n10:  *\\n11:  * Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\\n12:  * distributed under the License is distributed on an \\\"AS IS\\\" BASIS,\\n13:  * WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\\n14:  * See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\\n15:  * limitations under the License.\\n16:  */\\n17: package org.apache.kafka.common.security;\\n18: \\n19: import org.apache.kafka.common.config.SaslConfigs;\\n20: import org.apache.kafka.common.config.types.Password;\\n21: import org.apache.kafka.common.network.ListenerName;\\n22: \\n23: import org.slf4j.Logger;\\n24: import org.slf4j.LoggerFactory;\\n25: \\n26: import java.util.Arrays;\\n27: import java.util.List;\\n28: import java.util.Locale;\\n29: import java.util.Map;\\n30: import java.util.Set;\\n31: import java.util.stream.Collectors;\\n32: \\n33: import javax.security.auth.login.AppConfigurationEntry;\\n34: import javax.security.auth.login.Configuration;\\n35: \\n36: import static org.apache.kafka.common.security.JaasUtils.DISALLOWED_LOGIN_MODULES_CONFIG;\\n37: import static org.apache.kafka.common.security.JaasUtils.DISALLOWED_LOGIN_MODULES_DEFAULT;\\n38: \\n39: public class JaasContext {\\n40: \\n41:     private static final Logger LOG = LoggerFactory.getLogger(JaasContext.class);\\n42: \\n43:     private static final String GLOBAL_CONTEXT_NAME_SERVER = \\\"KafkaServer\\\";\\n44:     private static final String GLOBAL_CONTEXT_NAME_CLIENT = \\\"KafkaClient\\\";\\n45: \\n46:     /**\\n47:      * Returns an instance of this class.\\n48:      *\\n49:      * The context will contain the configuration specified by the JAAS configuration property\\n50:      * {@link SaslConfigs#SASL_JAAS_CONFIG} with prefix `listener.name.{listenerName}.{mechanism}.`\\n51:      * with listenerName and mechanism in lower case. The context `KafkaServer` will be returned\\n52:      * with a single login context entry loaded from the property.\\n53:      * <p>\\n54:      * If the property is not defined, the context will contain the default Configuration and\\n55:      * the context name will be one of:\\n56:      * <ol>\\n57:      *   <li>Lowercased listener name followed by a period and the string `KafkaServer`</li>\\n58:      *   <li>The string `KafkaServer`</li>\\n59:      *  </ol>\\n60:      * If both are valid entries in the default JAAS configuration, the first option is chosen.\\n61:      * </p>\\n62:      *\\n63:      * @throws IllegalArgumentException if listenerName or mechanism is not defined.\\n64:      */\\n65:     public static JaasContext loadServerContext(ListenerName listenerName, String mechanism, Map<String, ?> configs) {\\n66:         if (listenerName == null)\\n67:             throw new IllegalArgumentException(\\\"listenerName should not be null for SERVER\\\");\\n68:         if (mechanism == null)\\n69:             throw new IllegalArgumentException(\\\"mechanism should not be null for SERVER\\\");\\n70:         String listenerContextName = listenerName.value().toLowerCase(Locale.ROOT) + \\\".\\\" + GLOBAL_CONTEXT_NAME_SERVER;\\n71:         Password dynamicJaasConfig = (Password) configs.get(mechanism.toLowerCase(Locale.ROOT) + \\\".\\\" + SaslConfigs.SASL_JAAS_CONFIG);\\n72:         if (dynamicJaasConfig == null && configs.get(SaslConfigs.SASL_JAAS_CONFIG) != null)\\n73:             LOG.warn(\\\"Server config {} should be prefixed with SASL mechanism name, ignoring config\\\", SaslConfigs.SASL_JAAS_CONFIG);\\n74:         return load(Type.SERVER, listenerContextName, GLOBAL_CONTEXT_NAME_SERVER, dynamicJaasConfig);\\n75:     }\\n76: \\n77:     /**\\n78:      * Returns an instance of this class.\\n79:      *\\n80:      * If JAAS configuration property {@link SaslConfigs#SASL_JAAS_CONFIG} is specified,\\n81:      * the configuration object is created by parsing the property value. Otherwise, the default Configuration\\n82:      * is returned. The context name is always `KafkaClient`.\\n83:      *\\n84:      */\\n85:     public static JaasContext loadClientContext(Map<String, ?> configs) {\\n86:         Password dynamicJaasConfig = (Password) configs.get(SaslConfigs.SASL_JAAS_CONFIG);\\n87:         return load(JaasContext.Type.CLIENT, null, GLOBAL_CONTEXT_NAME_CLIENT, dynamicJaasConfig);\\n88:     }\\n89: \\n90:     static JaasContext load(JaasContext.Type contextType, String listenerContextName,\\n91:                             String globalContextName, Password dynamicJaasConfig) {\\n92:         if (dynamicJaasConfig != null) {\\n93:             JaasConfig jaasConfig = new JaasConfig(globalContextName, dynamicJaasConfig.value());\\n94:             AppConfigurationEntry[] contextModules = jaasConfig.getAppConfigurationEntry(globalContextName);\\n95:             if (contextModules == null || contextModules.length == 0)\\n96:                 throw new IllegalArgumentException(\\\"JAAS config property does not contain any login modules\\\");\\n97:             else if (contextModules.length != 1)\\n98:                 throw new IllegalArgumentException(\\\"JAAS config property contains \\\" + contextModules.length + \\\" login modules, should be 1 module\\\");\\n99: \\n100:             throwIfLoginModuleIsNotAllowed(contextModules[0]);\\n101:             return new JaasContext(globalContextName, contextType, jaasConfig, dynamicJaasConfig);\\n102:         } else\\n103:             return defaultContext(contextType, listenerContextName, globalContextName);\\n104:     }\\n105: \\n106:     private static void throwIfLoginModuleIsNotAllowed(AppConfigurationEntry appConfigurationEntry) {\\n107:         Set<String> disallowedLoginModuleList = Arrays.stream(\\n108:                 System.getProperty(DISALLOWED_LOGIN_MODULES_CONFIG, DISALLOWED_LOGIN_MODULES_DEFAULT).split(\\\",\\\"))\\n109:                 .map(String::trim)\\n110:                 .collect(Collectors.toSet());\\n111:         String loginModuleName = appConfigurationEntry.getLoginModuleName().trim();\\n112:         if (disallowedLoginModuleList.contains(loginModuleName)) {\\n113:             throw new IllegalArgumentException(loginModuleName + \\\" is not allowed. Update System property '\\\"\\n114:                     + DISALLOWED_LOGIN_MODULES_CONFIG + \\\"' to allow \\\" + loginModuleName);\\n115:         }\\n116:     }\\n117: \\n118:     private static JaasContext defaultContext(JaasContext.Type contextType, String listenerContextName,\\n119:                                               String globalContextName) {\\n120:         String jaasConfigFile = System.getProperty(JaasUtils.JAVA_LOGIN_CONFIG_PARAM);\\n121:         if (jaasConfigFile == null) {\\n122:             if (contextType == Type.CLIENT) {\\n123:                 LOG.debug(\\\"System property '\\\" + JaasUtils.JAVA_LOGIN_CONFIG_PARAM + \\\"' and Kafka SASL property '\\\" +\\n124:                         SaslConfigs.SASL_JAAS_CONFIG + \\\"' are not set, using default JAAS configuration.\\\");\\n125:             } else {\\n126:                 LOG.debug(\\\"System property '\\\" + JaasUtils.JAVA_LOGIN_CONFIG_PARAM + \\\"' is not set, using default JAAS \\\" +\\n127:                         \\\"configuration.\\\");\\n128:             }\\n129:         }\\n130: \\n131:         Configuration jaasConfig = Configuration.getConfiguration();\\n132: \\n133:         AppConfigurationEntry[] configEntries = null;\\n134:         String contextName = globalContextName;\\n135: \\n136:         if (listenerContextName != null) {\\n137:             configEntries = jaasConfig.getAppConfigurationEntry(listenerContextName);\\n138:             if (configEntries != null)\\n139:                 contextName = listenerContextName;\\n140:         }\\n141: \\n142:         if (configEntries == null)\\n143:             configEntries = jaasConfig.getAppConfigurationEntry(globalContextName);\\n144: \\n145:         if (configEntries == null) {\\n146:             String listenerNameText = listenerContextName == null ? \\\"\\\" : \\\" or '\\\" + listenerContextName + \\\"'\\\";\\n147:             String errorMessage = \\\"Could not find a '\\\" + globalContextName + \\\"'\\\" + listenerNameText + \\\" entry in the JAAS \\\" +\\n148:                     \\\"configuration. System property '\\\" + JaasUtils.JAVA_LOGIN_CONFIG_PARAM + \\\"' is \\\" +\\n149:                     (jaasConfigFile == null ? \\\"not set\\\" : jaasConfigFile);\\n150:             throw new IllegalArgumentException(errorMessage);\\n151:         }\\n152: \\n153:         for (AppConfigurationEntry appConfigurationEntry : configEntries) {\\n154:             throwIfLoginModuleIsNotAllowed(appConfigurationEntry);\\n155:         }\\n156:         return new JaasContext(contextName, contextType, jaasConfig, null);\\n157:     }\\n158: \\n159:     /**\\n160:      * The type of the SASL login context, it should be SERVER for the broker and CLIENT for the clients (consumer, producer,\\n161:      * etc.). This is used to validate behaviour (e.g. some functionality is only available in the broker or clients).\\n162:      */\\n163:     public enum Type { CLIENT, SERVER }\\n164: \\n165:     private final String name;\\n166:     private final Type type;\\n167:     private final Configuration configuration;\\n168:     private final List<AppConfigurationEntry> configurationEntries;\\n169:     private final Password dynamicJaasConfig;\\n170: \\n171:     public JaasContext(String name, Type type, Configuration configuration, Password dynamicJaasConfig) {\\n172:         this.name = name;\\n173:         this.type = type;\\n174:         this.configuration = configuration;\\n175:         AppConfigurationEntry[] entries = configuration.getAppConfigurationEntry(name);\\n176:         if (entries == null)\\n177:             throw new IllegalArgumentException(\\\"Could not find a '\\\" + name + \\\"' entry in this JAAS configuration.\\\");\\n178:         this.configurationEntries = List.of(entries);\\n179:         this.dynamicJaasConfig = dynamicJaasConfig;\\n180:     }\\n181: \\n182:     public String name() {\\n183:         return name;\\n184:     }\\n185: \\n186:     public Type type() {\\n187:         return type;\\n188:     }\\n189: \\n190:     public Configuration configuration() {\\n191:         return configuration;\\n192:     }\\n193: \\n194:     public List<AppConfigurationEntry> configurationEntries() {\\n195:         return configurationEntries;\\n196:     }\\n197: \\n198:     public Password dynamicJaasConfig() {\\n199:         return dynamicJaasConfig;\\n200:     }\\n201: \\n202:     /**\\n203:      * Returns the configuration option for <code>key</code> from this context.\\n204:      * If login module name is specified, return option value only from that module.\\n205:      */\\n206:     public static String configEntryOption(List<AppConfigurationEntry> configurationEntries, String key, String loginModuleName) {\\n207:         for (AppConfigurationEntry entry : configurationEntries) {\\n208:             if (loginModuleName != null && !loginModuleName.equals(entry.getLoginModuleName()))\\n209:                 continue;\\n210:             Object val = entry.getOptions().get(key);\\n211:             if (val != null)\\n212:                 return (String) val;\\n213:         }\\n214:         return null;\\n215:     }\\n216: \\n217: }\\n218: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/kafka--0753c489@31d9200189f81283bf60e929ddc38fd68ac034e2/-/blob/clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/security/JaasContext.java\"}"

cluster. \\\"\\n48:                                                         + \\\"Clients use this list to bootstrap and discover the full set of Kafka brokers. \\\"\\n49:                                                         + \\\"While the order of servers in the list does not matter, we recommend including more than one server to ensure resilience if any servers are down. \\\"\\n50:                                                         + \\\"This list does not need to contain the entire set of brokers, as Kafka clients automatically manage and update connections to the cluster {\\n351:                 Utils.maybeCloseQuietly(o, \\\"Instance of class \\\" + o.getClass().getName() + \\\" failed call to configure()\\\");\\n352:                 LOG.warn(\\n353:     public void testTasksFailOnInabilityToFence() throws Exception {\\n634:         brokerProps.put(SocketServerConfigs.LISTENER_SECURITY_PROTOCOL_MAP_CONFIG, \\\"CONTROLLER:SASL_PLAINTEXT,EXTERNAL:SASL_PLAINTEXT\\\");\\n635:         brokerProps.put(ServerConfigs.AUTHORIZER_CLASS_NAME_CONFIG, \\\"org.apache.kafka.metadata.authorizer.StandardAuthorizer\\\");\\n636:         brokerProps.put(BrokerSecurityConfigs.SASL_ENABLED_MECHANISMS_CONFIG, \\\"PLAIN\\\");\\n637:         brokerProps.put(BrokerSecurityConfigs.SASL_MECHANISM_INTER_BROKER_PROTOCOL_CONFIG, \\\"PLAIN\\\");\\n638:         brokerProps.put(KRaftConfigs.SASL_MECHANISM_CONTROLLER_PROTOCOL_CONFIG, \\\"PLAIN\\\");\\n639:         String listenerSaslJaasConfig = \\\"org.apache.kafka.common.security.plain.PlainLoginModule required \\\"\\n640:                 + \\\"username=\\\\\\\"super\\\\\\\" \\\"\\n641: \",\"endLine\":641,\"startLine\":633},{\"content\":\"196:         // Connector will return null from SourceConnector::exactlyOnceSupport\\n197:         props.put(CUSTOM_EXACTLY_ONCE_SUPPORT_CONFIG, TestableSourceConnector.EXACTLY_ONCE_NULL);\\n198:         ConfigInfos validation = connect.validateConnectorConfig(TestableSourceConnector.class.getSimpleName(), props);\\n199:         assertEquals(1, validation.errorCount(),\\n200:                 \\\"Preflight validation should have exactly one error\\\");\\n201:         ConfigInfo propertyValidation = findConfigInfo(EXACTLY_ONCE_SUPPORT_CONFIG, validation);\\n202:         assertFalse(propertyValidation.configValue().errors().isEmpty(),\\n203:                 \\\"Preflight validation for exactly-once support property should have at least one error message\\\");\\n204: \\n205: \",\"endLine\":205,\"startLine\":196},{\"content\":\"992: \\n993:     private ConfigInfo findConfigInfo(String property, ConfigInfos validationResult) {\\n994:         return validationResult.values().stream()\\n995:                 .filter(info -> property.equals(info.configKey().name()))\\n996:                 .findAny()\\n997:                 .orElseThrow(() -> new AssertionError(\\\"Failed to find configuration validation result for property '\\\" + property + \\\"'\\\"));\\n998:     }\\n999: \",\"endLine\":999,\"startLine\":992}],\"file\":\"connect/runtime/src/test/java/org/apache/kafka/connect/integration/ExactlyOnceSourceIntegrationTest.java\",\"repo\":\"github.com/sg-evals/kafka--0753c489\",\"type\":\"file\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/kafka--0753c489/-/blob/connect/runtime/src/test/java/org/apache/kafka/connect/integration/ExactlyOnceSourceIntegrationTest.java\"}]}"

org.apache.kafka.common.config.SslClientAuth;\\n23: import org.apache.kafka.common.config.SslConfigs;\\n24: import org.apache.kafka.common.security.auth.KafkaPrincipalBuilder;\\n25: import java.util.List;\\n30: \\n31: import static org.apache.kafka.common.config.ConfigDef.Importance.LOW;\\n32: import static org.apache.kafka.common.config.ConfigDef.Importance.MEDIUM;\\n33: import static org.apache.kafka.common.config.ConfigDef.Type.BOOLEAN;\\n34: import static org.apache.kafka.common.config.ConfigDef.Type.CLASS;\\n35: import static org.apache.kafka.common.config.ConfigDef.Type.DOUBLE;\\n36: import static org.apache.kafka.common.config.ConfigDef.Type.INT;\\n37: import static org.apache.kafka.common.config.ConfigDef.Type.PASSWORD;\\n40: import static org.apache.kafka.common.config.ConfigDef.Type.SHORT;\\n41: import static org.apache.kafka.common.config.ConfigDef.Type.STRING;\\n42: \\n43: /**\\n44:  * Common home for broker-side security configs which need to be accessible from the libraries shared\\n45:  * between the broker and the client.\\n46:  *\\n47:  * Note this is an internal API and subject to change without notice.\\n48:  */\\n49: public class BrokerSecurityConfigs {\\n50: \\n51:     public static final String PRINCIPAL_BUILDER_CLASS_CONFIG = \\\"principal.builder.class\\\";\\n52: \\n53:     public static final String SSL_PRINCIPAL_MAPPING_RULES_CONFIG = \\\"ssl.principal.mapping.rules\\\";\\n54:     public static final String DEFAULT_SSL_PRINCIPAL_MAPPING_RULES = \\\"DEFAULT\\\";\\n55:     public static final String SSL_PRINCIPAL_MAPPING_RULES_DOC = \\\"A list of rules for mapping from distinguished name\\\" +\\n56:             \\\" from the client certificate to short name. The rules are evaluated in order and the first rule that matches\\\" +\\n57:             \\\" a principal name is used to map it to a short name. Any later rules in the list are ignored. By default,\\\" +\\n58:             \\\" distinguished name of the X.500 certificate will be the principal. For more details on the format please\\\" +\\n59:             \\\" see <a href=\\\\\\\"#security_authz\\\\\\\"> security authorization and acls</a>. Note that this configuration is ignored\\\" +\\n60:             \\\" if an extension of KafkaPrincipalBuilder is provided by the <code>\\\" + PRINCIPAL_BUILDER_CLASS_CONFIG + \\\"</code>\\\" +\\n61:             \\\" configuration.\\\";\\n62: \\n63:     public static final String SASL_KERBEROS_PRINCIPAL_TO_LOCAL_RULES_CONFIG = \\\"sasl.kerberos.principal.to.local.rules\\\";\\n64:     public static final List<String> DEFAULT_SASL_KERBEROS_PRINCIPAL_TO_LOCAL_RULES = Collections.singletonList(DEFAULT_SSL_PRINCIPAL_MAPPING_RULES);\\n65:     public static final String SASL_KERBEROS_PRINCIPAL_TO_LOCAL_RULES_DOC = \\\"A list of rules for mapping from principal \\\" +\\n66:             \\\"names to short names (typically operating system usernames). The rules are evaluated in order and the \\\" +\\n67:             \\\"first rule that matches a principal name is used to map it to a short name. Any later rules in the list are \\\" +\\n68:             \\\"ignored. By default, principal names of the form <code>{username}/{hostname}@{REALM}</code> are mapped \\\" +\\n69:             \\\"to <code>{username}</code>. For more details on the format please see <a href=\\\\\\\"#security_authz\\\\\\\"> \\\" +\\n70:             \\\"security authorization and acls</a>. Note that this configuration is ignored if an extension of \\\" +\\n71:             \\\"<code>KafkaPrincipalBuilder</code> is provided by the <code>\\\" + PRINCIPAL_BUILDER_CLASS_CONFIG + \\\"</code> configuration.\\\";\\n72: \\n73:     public static final Class<? extends KafkaPrincipalBuilder> PRINCIPAL_BUILDER_CLASS_DEFAULT = DefaultKafkaPrincipalBuilder.class;\\n74:     public static final String PRINCIPAL_BUILDER_CLASS_DOC = \\\"The fully qualified name of a class that implements the \\\" +\\n75:             \\\"KafkaPrincipalBuilder interface, which is used to build the KafkaPrincipal object used during \\\" +\\n76:             \\\"authorization. If no principal builder is defined, the default behavior depends \\\" +\\n77:             \\\"on the security protocol in use. For SSL authentication,  the principal will be derived using the \\\" +\\n78:             \\\"rules defined by <code>\\\" + SSL_PRINCIPAL_MAPPING_RULES_CONFIG + \\\"</code> applied on the distinguished \\\" +\\n79:             \\\"name from the client certificate if one is provided; otherwise, if client authentication is not required, \\\" +\\n80:             \\\"the principal name will be ANONYMOUS. For SASL authentication, the principal will be derived using the \\\" +\\n81:             \\\"rules defined by <code>\\\" + SASL_KERBEROS_PRINCIPAL_TO_LOCAL_RULES_CONFIG + \\\"</code> if GSSAPI is in use, \\\" +\\n82:             \\\"and the SASL authentication ID for other mechanisms. For PLAINTEXT, the principal will be ANONYMOUS. \\\" +\\n83:             \\\"Note that custom implementations of <code>KafkaPrincipalBuilder</code> is required to implement <code>KafkaPrincipalSerde</code> \\\" +\\n84:             \\\"interface, otherwise brokers will not be able to forward requests to the controller.\\\";\\n85: \\n86:     public static final String SSL_CLIENT_AUTH_CONFIG = \\\"ssl.client.auth\\\";\\n87:     public static final String SSL_CLIENT_AUTH_DEFAULT = SslClientAuth.NONE.toString();\\n88:     public static final String SSL_CLIENT_AUTH_DOC = \\\"Configures kafka broker to request client authentication.\\\"\\n89:             + \\\" The following settings are common: \\\"\\n90:             + \\\" <ul>\\\"\\n91:             + \\\" <li><code>ssl.client.auth=required</code> If set to required client authentication is required.\\\"\\n92:             + \\\" <li><code>ssl.client.auth=requested</code> This means client authentication is optional.\\\"\\n93:             + \\\" unlike required, if this option is set client can choose not to provide authentication information about itself\\\"\\n94:             + \\\" <li><code>ssl.client.auth=none</code> This means client authentication is not needed.\\\"\\n95:             + \\\"</ul>\\\";\\n96: \\n97:     public static final String SASL_ENABLED_MECHANISMS_CONFIG = \\\"sasl.enabled.mechanisms\\\";\\n98:     public static final List<String> DEFAULT_SASL_ENABLED_MECHANISMS = Collections.singletonList(SaslConfigs.GSSAPI_MECHANISM);\\n99:     public static final String SASL_ENABLED_MECHANISMS_DOC = \\\"The list of SASL mechanisms enabled in the Kafka server. \\\"\\n100:             + \\\"The list may contain any mechanism for which a security provider is available. \\\"\\n101:             + \\\"Only GSSAPI is enabled by default.\\\";\\n102: \\n103:     public static final String SASL_SERVER_CALLBACK_HANDLER_CLASS_CONFIG = \\\"sasl.server.callback.handler.class\\\";\\n104:     public static final String SASL_SERVER_CALLBACK_HANDLER_CLASS_DOC = \\\"The fully qualified name of a SASL server callback handler \\\"\\n105:             + \\\"class that implements the AuthenticateCallbackHandler interface. Server callback handlers must be prefixed with \\\"\\n106:             + \\\"listener prefix and SASL mechanism name in lower-case. For example, \\\"\\n107:             + \\\"listener.name.sasl_ssl.plain.sasl.server.callback.handler.class=com.example.CustomPlainCallbackHandler.\\\";\\n108: \\n109:     public static final String CONNECTIONS_MAX_REAUTH_MS_CONFIG = \\\"connections.max.reauth.ms\\\";\\n110:     public static final long DEFAULT_CONNECTIONS_MAX_REAUTH_MS = 0L;\\n111:     public static final String CONNECTIONS_MAX_REAUTH_MS_DOC = \\\"When explicitly set to a positive number (the default is 0, not a positive number), \\\"\\n112:             + \\\"a session lifetime that will not exceed the configured value will be communicated to v2.2.0 or later clients when they authenticate. \\\"\\n113:             + \\\"The broker will disconnect any such connection that is not re-authenticated within the session lifetime and that is then subsequently \\\"\\n114:             + \\\"used for any purpose other than re-authentication. Configuration names can optionally be prefixed with listener prefix and SASL \\\"\\n115:             + \\\"mechanism name in lower-case. For example, listener.name.sasl_ssl.oauthbearer.connections.max.reauth.ms=3600000\\\";\\n116: \\n117:     public static final String SASL_SERVER_MAX_RECEIVE_SIZE_CONFIG = \\\"sasl.server.max.receive.size\\\";\\n118:     public static final int DEFAULT_SASL_SERVER_MAX_RECEIVE_SIZE = 524288;\\n119:     public static final String SASL_SERVER_MAX_RECEIVE_SIZE_DOC = \\\"The maximum receive size allowed before and during initial SASL authentication.\\\" +\\n120:             \\\" Default receive size is 512KB. GSSAPI limits requests to 64K, but we allow upto 512KB by default for custom SASL mechanisms. In practice,\\\" +\\n121:             \\\" PLAIN, SCRAM and OAUTH mechanisms can use much smaller limits.\\\";\\n122: \\n123:     public static final String SSL_ALLOW_DN_CHANGES_CONFIG = \\\"ssl.allow.dn.changes\\\";\\n124:     public static final boolean DEFAULT_SSL_ALLOW_DN_CHANGES_VALUE = false;\\n125:     public static final String SSL_ALLOW_DN_CHANGES_DOC = \\\"Indicates whether changes to the certificate distinguished name should be allowed during\\\" +\\n126:             \\\" a dynamic reconfiguration of certificates or not.\\\";\\n127: \\n128:     public static final String SSL_ALLOW_SAN_CHANGES_CONFIG = \\\"ssl.allow.san.changes\\\";\\n129:     public static final boolean DEFAULT_SSL_ALLOW_SAN_CHANGES_VALUE = false;\\n130:     public static final String SSL_ALLOW_SAN_CHANGES_DOC = \\\"Indicates whether changes to the certificate subject alternative names should be allowed during \\\" +\\n131:             \\\"a dynamic reconfiguration of certificates or not.\\\";\\n132: \\n133:     public static final String SASL_MECHANISM_INTER_BROKER_PROTOCOL_CONFIG = \\\"sasl.mechanism.inter.broker.protocol\\\";\\n134:     public static final String SASL_MECHANISM_INTER_BROKER_PROTOCOL_DOC = \\\"SASL mechanism used for inter-broker communication. Default is GSSAPI.\\\";\\n135: \\n136:     // The allowlist of the SASL OAUTHBEARER endpoints\\n137:     public static final String ALLOWED_SASL_OAUTHBEARER_URLS_CONFIG = \\\"org.apache.kafka.sasl.oauthbearer.allowed.urls\\\";\\n138:     public static final String ALLOWED_SASL_OAUTHBEARER_URLS_DEFAULT = \\\"\\\";\\n139: \\n140:     public static final String "{\"content\":\"296: \\n297:     public static void postValidateSaslMechanismConfig(AbstractConfig config) {\\n298:         SecurityProtocol securityProtocol = SecurityProtocol.forName(config.getString(CommonClientConfigs.SECURITY_PROTOCOL_CONFIG));\\n299:         String clientSaslMechanism = config.getString(SaslConfigs.SASL_MECHANISM);\\n300:         if (securityProtocol == SecurityProtocol.SASL_PLAINTEXT || securityProtocol == SecurityProtocol.SASL_SSL) {\\n301:             if (clientSaslMechanism == null || clientSaslMechanism.isEmpty()) {\\n302:                 throw new ConfigException(SaslConfigs.SASL_MECHANISM, null, \\\"When the \\\" + CommonClientConfigs.SECURITY_PROTOCOL_CONFIG +\\n303:                         \\\" configuration enables SASL, mechanism must be non-null and non-empty string.\\\");\\n304:             }\\n305:         }\\n306:     }\\n307: \\n308:     public static List<MetricsReporter> metricsReporters(AbstractConfig config) {\\n309:         return metricsReporters(Collections.emptyMap(), config);\\n310:     }\\n311: \\n312:     public static List<MetricsReporter> metricsReporters(String clientId, AbstractConfig config) {\\n313:         return metricsReporters(Collections.singletonMap(CommonClientConfigs.CLIENT_ID_CONFIG, clientId), config);\\n314:     }\\n315: \\n316: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/kafka--0753c489@31d9200189f81283bf60e929ddc38fd68ac034e2/-/blob/clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/clients/CommonClientConfigs.java%3FL296-315\"}"

extends AuthenticateCallbackHandler> clazz = (Class<? extends AuthenticateCallbackHandler>) configs.get(SaslConfigs.SASL_CLIENT_CALLBACK_HANDLER_CLASS);\\n311:         if (clazz == null)\\n312:             clazz = clientCallbackHandlerClass();\\n313:         AuthenticateCallbackHandler callbackHandler = Utils.newInstance(clazz);\\n314:         saslCallbackHandlers.put(clientSaslMechanism, callbackHandler);\\n315:     }\\n316: \\n317:     private void createServerCallbackHandlers(Map<String, ?> configs) {\\n318:         for (String mechanism : jaasContexts.keySet()) {\\n319:             AuthenticateCallbackHandler callbackHandler;\\n320:             String prefix = ListenerName.saslMechanismPrefix(mechanism);\\n321:             @SuppressWarnings(\\\"unchecked\\\")\\n322:             Class<? extends AuthenticateCallbackHandler> clazz =\\n323:                     (Class<? extends AuthenticateCallbackHandler>) configs.get(prefix + BrokerSecurityConfigs.SASL_SERVER_CALLBACK_HANDLER_CLASS_CONFIG);\\n324:             if (clazz != null)\\n325:                 callbackHandler = Utils.newInstance(clazz);\\n326:             else if (mechanism.equals(PlainSaslServer.PLAIN_MECHANISM))\\n327:                 callbackHandler = new PlainServerCallbackHandler();\\n328:             else if (ScramMechanism.isScram(mechanism))\\n329:                 callbackHandler = new ScramServerCallbackHandler(credentialCache.cache(mechanism, ScramCredential.class), tokenCache);\\n330:             else if (mechanism.equals(OAuthBearerLoginModule.OAUTHBEARER_MECHANISM))\\n331:                 callbackHandler = new OAuthBearerUnsecuredValidatorCallbackHandler();\\n332:             else\\n333:                 callbackHandler = new SaslServerCallbackHandler();\\n334:             saslCallbackHandlers.put(mechanism, callbackHandler);\\n335:         }\\n336:     }\\n337: \\n338:     private void createConnectionsMaxReauthMsMap(Map<String, ?> configs) {\\n339:         for (String mechanism : jaasContexts.keySet()) {\\n340:             String prefix = ListenerName.saslMechanismPrefix(mechanism);\\n341: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/kafka--0753c489@31d9200189f81283bf60e929ddc38fd68ac034e2/-/blob/clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/network/SaslChannelBuilder.java%3FL300-340\"}"

\"clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/config/internals/BrokerSecurityConfigs.java\",\n      \"symbol\": \"SASL_ENABLED_MECHANISMS_CONFIG\"\n    },\n    {\n      \"repo\": \"apache/kafka\",\n      \"path\": \"clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/config/internals/BrokerSecurityConfigs.java\",\n      \"symbol\": \"SASL_MECHANISM_INTER_BROKER_PROTOCOL_CONFIG\"\n    },\n    {\n      \"repo\": \"apache/kafka\",\n      \"path\": \"clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/security/authenticator/SaslServerAuthenticator.java\",\n      \"symbol\": \"SaslServerAuthenticator\"\n    },\n    {\n      \"repo\": \"apache/kafka\",\n      \"path\": \"clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/network/SaslChannelBuilder.java\",\n      \"symbol\": \"buildServerAuthenticator\"\n    },\n    {\n      \"repo\": \"apache/kafka\",\n      \"path\": \"clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/network/SaslChannelBuilder.java\",\n      \"symbol\": \"serverCallbackHandler\"\n    },\n    {\n      \"repo\": \"apache/kafka\",\n      \"path\": \"clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/network/ChannelBuilders.java\",\n      \"symbol\": \"serverChannelBuilder\"\n    },\n    {\n      \"repo\": \"apache/kafka\",\n      \"path\": \"clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/network/ChannelBuilders.java\",\n      \"symbol\": \"create\"\n    },\n    {\n      \"repo\": \"apache/kafka\",\n      \"path\": \"clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/clients/CommonClientConfigs.java\",\n      \"symbol\": \"postValidateSaslMechanismConfig\"\n    },\n    {\n      \"repo\": \"apache/kafka\",\n      \"path\": \"core/src/main/scala/kafka/server/KafkaConfig.scala\",\n      \"symbol\": \"validateInterBrokerSaslMechanism\"\n    },\n    {\n      \"repo\": \"apache/kafka\",\n      \"path\": \"clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/security/oauthbearer/internals/unsecured/OAuthBearerUnsecuredValidatorCallbackHandler.java\",\n      \"symbol\": \"configure\"\n    }\n  ],\n  \"chain\": [\n    {\n      \"repo\": \"apache/kafka\",\n      \"path\": \"clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/network/ChannelBuilders.java\",\n      \"symbol\": \"serverChannelBuilder\"\n    },\n    {\n      \"repo\": \"apache/kafka\",\n      \"path\": \"clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/network/ChannelBuilders.java\",\n      \"symbol\": \"create\"\n    },\n    {\n      \"repo\": \"apache/kafka\",\n      \"path\": \"clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/security/JaasContext.java\",\n      \"symbol\": \"loadServerContext\"\n    },\n    {\n      \"repo\": \"apache/kafka\",\n      \"path\": \"clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/security/JaasContext.java\",\n      \"symbol\": \"throwIfLoginModuleIsNotAllowed\"\n    },\n    {\n      \"repo\": \"apache/kafka\",\n      \"path\": \"clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/network/SaslChannelBuilder.java\",\n      \"symbol\": \"buildServerAuthenticator\"\n    },\n    {\n      \"repo\": \"apache/kafka\",\n      \"path\": \"clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/security/authenticator/SaslServerAuthenticator.java\",\n      \"symbol\": \"SaslServerAuthenticator\"\n    }\n  ],\n  \"text\": \"The Apache Kafka broker validates SASL mechanism configuration at startup through a multi-layer validation chain:\\n\\n1. **Configuration Definition and Validation (BrokerSecurityConfigs.java)**: The broker defines SASL configuration constants including `SASL_ENABLED_MECHANISMS_CONFIG` and `SASL_MECHANISM_INTER_BROKER_PROTOCOL_CONFIG` with type and importance specifications.\\n\\n2. **JAAS Configuration Loading and Validation (JaasContext.java)**: When the broker starts with SASL-enabled listeners, `JaasContext.loadServerContext()` is called for each enabled SASL mechanism. This method validates that:\\n   - Both listener name and mechanism are non-null (lines 66-69)\\n   - The JAAS configuration contains exactly one login module for dynamic configurations (lines 95-98)\\n   - All login modules are in the allowed list via `throwIfLoginModuleIsNotAllowed()` (lines 106-116)\\n   - Required JAAS configuration entries exist when using the default JAAS configuration (lines 145-150)\\n\\n3. **Channel Builder and Server Creation (ChannelBuilders.java, SaslChannelBuilder.java)**: When setting up SASL-enabled listeners in server mode, `ChannelBuilders.serverChannelBuilder()` calls the `create()` method which:\\n   - Retrieves the list of enabled mechanisms from `SASL_ENABLED_MECHANISMS_CONFIG` (line 137)\\n   - Creates a JAAS context for each enabled mechanism (line 140)\\n   - Instantiates `SaslChannelBuilder` with the validated JAAS contexts\\n\\n4. **Server Authenticator Validation (SaslServerAuthenticator.java)**: The `SaslServerAuthenticator` constructor validates that:\\n   - The `SASL_ENABLED_MECHANISMS_CONFIG` list is non-null and non-empty (lines 178-180)\\n   - For each enabled mechanism, a valid callback handler exists (lines 183-184)\\n   - A Subject is configured for each mechanism (lines 185-186)\\n   - Mechanism-specific requirements are met (GSSAPI Kerberos, SCRAM credentials, OAUTHBEARER validators)\\n\\n5. **Mechanism-Specific Validation**:\\n   - **PLAIN mechanism**: Uses PlainServerCallbackHandler which validates username/password configuration\\n   - **SCRAM mechanism**: Uses ScramServerCallbackHandler which validates SCRAM credential store\\n   - **OAUTHBEARER mechanism**: Uses OAuthBearerUnsecuredValidatorCallbackHandler/BrokerJwtValidator which validates JWT configuration and endpoints (ConfigurationUtils.java)\\n   - **GSSAPI mechanism**: Validates Kerberos service name and principal-to-local rules in SaslChannelBuilder\\n\\n6. **Inter-Broker Protocol Validation (KafkaConfig.scala)**: The broker validates that the configured `sasl.mechanism.inter.broker.protocol` is included in the `sasl.enabled.mechanisms` list, ensuring the inter-broker protocol uses a supported mechanism.\\n\\n7. **Dynamic Configuration Management (DynamicBrokerConfig.scala)**: Lists SASL configurations that are dynamically reconfigurable, including SASL_MECHANISM_INTER_BROKER_PROTOCOL_CONFIG and SASL_JAAS_CONFIG.\\n\\n8. **Client-Side Validation (CommonClientConfigs.java)**: When brokers act as clients for inter-broker communication, `postValidateSaslMechanismConfig()` ensures that when SASL_PLAINTEXT or SASL_SSL is configured, a non-empty SASL mechanism is specified.\\n\\nThe validation chain ensures that by the time the broker starts accepting client connections, all SASL mechanisms are properly configured with valid JAAS contexts, callback handlers, and mechanism-specific requirements are met.\"\n}\n",
  "file_path": "/workspace/answer.json"
}

Definition and Validation (BrokerSecurityConfigs.java)**: The broker defines SASL configuration constants including `SASL_ENABLED_MECHANISMS_CONFIG` and `SASL_MECHANISM_INTER_BROKER_PROTOCOL_CONFIG` with type and importance specifications.\\n\\n2. **JAAS Configuration Loading and Validation (JaasContext.java)**: When the broker starts with SASL-enabled listeners, `JaasContext.loadServerContext()` Retrieves the list of enabled mechanisms from `SASL_ENABLED_MECHANISMS_CONFIG` (line 137)\\n   - Creates a JAAS context for each enabled mechanism (line 140)\\n   - Instantiates `SaslChannelBuilder` with the validated JAAS contexts\\n\\n4. **Server Authenticator Validation (SaslServerAuthenticator.java)**: The `SaslServerAuthenticator` constructor validates that:\\n   - The `SASL_ENABLED_MECHANISMS_CONFIG` list is non-null and non-empty (lines 178-180)\\n   - For each enabled mechanism, a valid callback handler exists (lines 183-184)\\n   - A Subject is configured for each mechanism (lines 185-186)\\n   - Mechanism-specific requirements are met (GSSAPI Kerberos, SCRAM credentials, OAUTHBEARER validators)\\n\\n5. **Mechanism-Specific Validation**:\\n   - **PLAIN mechanism**: Uses PlainServerCallbackHandler which validates username/password configuration\\n   - **SCRAM mechanism**: Uses ScramServerCallbackHandler which validates SCRAM credential store\\n   - **OAUTHBEARER mechanism**: Uses OAuthBearerUnsecuredValidatorCallbackHandler/BrokerJwtValidator which validates JWT configuration and endpoints (ConfigurationUtils.java)\\n   - **GSSAPI mechanism**: Validates Kerberos service name and principal-to-local rules in SaslChannelBuilder\\n\\n6. **Inter-Broker Protocol Validation (KafkaConfig.scala)**: The broker validates that the configured `sasl.mechanism.inter.broker.protocol` is included in the `sasl.enabled.mechanisms` list, ensuring the inter-broker protocol uses a supported mechanism.\\n\\n7. **Dynamic Configuration Management (DynamicBrokerConfig.scala)**: Lists SASL configurations that are dynamically reconfigurable, including SASL_MECHANISM_INTER_BROKER_PROTOCOL_CONFIG and SASL_JAAS_CONFIG.\\n\\n8. **Client-Side Validation (CommonClientConfigs.java)**: When brokers act as clients for inter-broker communication, `postValidateSaslMechanismConfig()` ensures that when SASL_PLAINTEXT or SASL_SSL is configured, a non-empty SASL mechanism is specified.\\n\\nThe validation chain ensures that by the time the broker starts accepting client connections, all SASL mechanisms are properly configured with valid JAAS contexts, callback handlers, and mechanism-specific requirements are met.\"\n}\n",
    "filePath": "/workspace/answer.json",
    "numLines": 161,
    "startLine": 1,
    "totalLines": 161
  },
  "type": "text"
}
